text: ## Export WooCommerce Data – Advanced Topics

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Data to CSV, Excel, XML, or Google Sheets
heading_path: How to Filter Exported WooCommerce Data
text:
### How to Filter Exported WooCommerce Data

In Step 1, you can **Add Filtering Options** to export WooCommerce data selectively. You can filter the export based on all the fields recognized by WP All Export for your selected post type.

For example, when exporting WooCommerce orders, you can use the **Order Date** as the **Element**, a required **Rule** such as **newer than**, and the Value to filter by. Once done, click on **Add Rule**. When handling dates, you can use natural language. Here's an example showing how to export all orders made since yesterday:

![Export WooCommerce Data Add Filtering Options](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Export-WooCommerce-Data-Add-Filtering-Options-1024x299.jpg)

Our in-depth guide explains how to apply filters for an export here: [Filter Exported WordPress Data](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/filter-exported-wordpress-data/).

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Data to CSV, Excel, XML, or Google Sheets
heading_path: WooCommerce Data Not Exported Correctly
text:
### WooCommerce Data Not Exported Correctly

If some of the WooCommerce data is not exported correctly, the easiest way to understand what's wrong is to compare the data in the database with the exported data. If the two match, you can rule out any issues with WP All Export, as it's exporting the data correctly.

If the data doesn't match and you can't find the culprit, you can replicate everything on a sandbox environment ([https://www.wpallimport.com/debug/](https://www.wpallimport.com/debug/)) and then send us that site's URL so we can take a closer look. To do so, email us at [support@wpallimport.com](mailto:support@wpallimport.com) or via our [Support form](https://www.wpallimport.com/support/).

heading_path: Export WooCommerce Data – Frequently Asked Questions
text: ## Export WooCommerce Data – Frequently Asked Questions

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Data to CSV, Excel, XML, or Google Sheets
heading_path: How Do I Export WooCommerce Customers to CSV?
text:
### How Do I Export WooCommerce Customers to CSV?

1. Create a new export via **All Export  › New Export**.
2. Select **WooCommerce Customers** from the dropdown list.
3. Click **Customize Export File** and set up the export.
4. Configure and run the export.
5. Download the exported WooCommerce customers.

CSV is the default export format. You can learn more about exporting WooCommerce customers here: [How to Export WooCommerce Customers to CSV, Excel, or XML](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-export-woocommerce-customers-to-csv-or-xml/).

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Data to CSV, Excel, XML, or Google Sheets
heading_path: How Do I Export Products From WooCommerce to XML?
text:
### How Do I Export Products From WooCommerce to XML?

1. Create a new export via **All Export › New Export**.
2. Choose **WooCommerce Products** from the list.
3. **Customize Export File** and define the export columns.
4. Change the export format to an XML feed.
5. Configure and run the products export.
6. Download the exported WooCommerce products.

You can learn more about exporting products here: [How to Export WooCommerce Products to CSV, Excel, XML, or Google Sheets](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-export-woocommerce-products-to-csv-or-xml/).

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Data to CSV, Excel, XML, or Google Sheets
heading_path: How Do I Schedule a WooCommerce Data Export?
text:
### How Do I Schedule a WooCommerce Data Export?

You can schedule exports using the options included with WP All Export, which are:

1. Automatic Scheduling Service: an optional and monthly paid service that can be set up directly from within WP All Export's interface. You can learn more about this service here: [Run WordPress Export on a Schedule](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/run-wordpress-export-on-a-schedule/).
2. Manual Scheduling: manual cron jobs that can be set up via your web host (i.e., in cPanel or Plesk). You can learn more about cron jobs here: [Schedule WordPress Exports Using Cron Jobs](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/scheduling-exports-using-cron-jobs/).

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Data to CSV, Excel, XML, or Google Sheets
heading_path: Can I Export Any Type of WooCommerce Data?
text:
### Can I Export Any Type of WooCommerce Data?

Yes, WP All Export can export any WooCommerce data to CSV, Excel, XML, or Google Sheets. You can export all your WooCommerce products or use filters to select a few. You can also export your WooCommerce orders or get a list of all your customers.

You can export the existing reviews on your WooCommerce products or the WooCommerce coupons that exist on your site. WP All Export can also create a [Google Merchant Center Product Feed](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-export-woocommerce-to-google-merchant-center/) with all your WooCommerce products.

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Data to CSV, Excel, XML, or Google Sheets
heading_path: What Add-Ons Do I Need to Export WooCommerce Orders?
text:
### What Add-Ons Do I Need to Export WooCommerce Orders?

To export WooCommerce orders, you need to have the WooCommerce Export Add-On active on your site, along with WP All Export.

text: To import WordPress comments, go to **All Import › New Import**, provide your import file, and map the incoming data elements to the relevant WordPress comment fields. Run the import, then verify that the new data is correct.

page_title: How to Import WordPress Comments
heading_path: Step 1: Create a New Import for WordPress Comments
text:
## Step 1: Create a New Import for WordPress Comments

Navigate to **All Import › New Import** on your site, provide your import file (upload, download, or select it), then choose **Comments** from the dropdown list:

![Import WordPress Comments New Import](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Import-WordPress-Comments-New-Import-1024x734.jpg)

Click **Continue to Step 2**.

page_title: How to Import WordPress Comments
heading_path: Step 2: Review the Import File
text:
## Step 2: Review the Import File

The **Review Import File** screen appears next. Here, check that your import file and data are correct:

![Import WordPress Comments Review Import File](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Import-WordPress-Comments-Review-Import-File-1024x999.jpg)

Near the bottom, you can use the **Manage Filtering Options** section to apply filters to the import data, which you to narrow down which comments to import.

Click **Continue to Step 3**.

page_title: How to Import WordPress Comments
heading_path: Step 3: Map the Incoming Data Elements to the Correct Fields
text:
## Step 3: Map the Incoming Data Elements to the Correct Fields

Next, you will see the **Drag & Drop** screen, which allows you to map the data elements from your import file to their corresponding **Comments** target fields. To do so, drag the import elements from the right side of the screen to their target fields on the left.

page_title: How to Import WordPress Comments
heading_path: Comment Content
text:
### Comment Content

First, map the **Comment** itself, then the **Parent Post** it's attached to (with post ID, slug, or post title), then the **Comment Date**:

![Import WordPress Comments Comment Content](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Import-WordPress-Comments-Comment-Content-1024x690.jpg)

The **Advanced Options** section lets you define **Approval Status**, **Comment Type**, **Karma,** and **Parent Comment** (matched by comment ID or date/time of parent in GMT time).

![Import WordPress Comments Comment Content Advanced Options](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Import-WordPress-Comments-Comment-Content-Advanced-Options-1024x415.jpg)

page_title: How to Import WordPress Comments
heading_path: Comment Author
text:
### Comment Author

This section lets you map the **Author Name** and **Author Email**:

![Import WordPress Comments Content Author](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Import-WordPress-Comments-Content-Author-1024x632.jpg)

There are additional advanced options in this section that let you map the **Author User ID**, **Author IP**, **Author URL**, and **Comment Agent**:

![Import WordPress Comments Content Author Advanced Options](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Import-WordPress-Comments-Content-Author-Advanced-Options-1024x389.jpg)

page_title: How to Import WordPress Comments
heading_path: Comment Meta
text:
### Comment Meta

This section lets you add custom fields or meta to your comments:

![Import WordPress Comments Comment Meta](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Import-WordPress-Comments-Comment-Meta-1024x727.jpg)

Below, you may see other sections depending on your installed add-ons, as well as the **Function Editor** at the end.

Click **Continue to Step 4**.

page_title: How to Import WordPress Comments
heading_path: Step 4: Configure the Import Settings and Run the Import
text:
## Step 4: Configure the Import Settings and Run the Import

You now see the **Import Settings** screen, where you can define key import settings. To start, click **Auto-detect** to generate a unique identifier for the imported comments. This allows our software to identify duplicates and properly apply updates.

![Import WordPress Comments Import Settings](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Import-WordPress-Comments-Import-Settings-1024x777.jpg)

Below this, you'll see the **Scheduling Options** and **Configure Advanced Settings** sections. These can be left with their default selections for this example.

Click **Continue** at the bottom to move to the next step.

This brings you to the **Confirm & Run** interface, where you can review an import summary explaining what will happen. Click on **Confirm & Run Import** to proceed.

Once the import completes, you'll see this screen:

![Import WordPress Comments Import Complete](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Import-WordPress-Comments-Import-Complete-1024x868.jpg)

Check and view the comments after you import the WordPress comments.

page_title: How to Import WordPress Comments
heading_path: Comment Skipped Due to Post Match Not Found
text:
### Comment Skipped Due to Post Match Not Found

If a comment is skipped because no post parent is found, you'll see a message like this in the History Logs:

```
[01:44:36] SKIPPED: Comment post not found by id `Hello world!`
```

To solve this, you need to check that there's a valid post matching the post ID, title, or slug that you're using. Comments can only be imported when they have a matching post.

Learn more about importing WordPress posts here: [How to Import WordPress Posts](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-import-wordpress-posts/).

page_title: How to Import WordPress Comments
heading_path: Use Filters to Select What Comments to Import
text:
### Use Filters to Select What Comments to Import

You can filter the comments to import during Step 2 or when editing the existing import template under **Manage Filtering Options**. To create a filter, you need to specify an **Element**, a **Rule**, and a **Value**.

![Import WordPress Comments Manage Filtering Options](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Import-WordPress-Comments-Manage-Filtering-Options-1024x266.jpg)

In this screenshot, we've created a rule that will only import comments where the parent post ID has a value equal to 8.

To change a filter for an existing import, locate that import in **All Import ›** **Manage Imports**, go to **Edit Template**, and modify the filters in **Manage Filtering Options**.

You can learn more here: [Filtering with XPath](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/filtering-with-xpath/).

page_title: How to Import WordPress Comments
heading_path: Import WordPress Comments – Frequently Asked Questions
text: ## Import WordPress Comments – Frequently Asked Questions

page_title: How to Import WordPress Comments
heading_path: How Do I Enable Comments for WordPress?
text:
### How Do I Enable Comments for WordPress?

1. Go to **Settings › Discussion** in the main WordPress menu.
2. Check the **Allow people to submit comments on new posts** option.
3. Configure options in the **Other comment settings** section.
4. Configure options in the **Before a comment appears** section.
5. Configure options in the **Comment Moderation** section.
6. Enter restricted comment words in the **Disallowed Comment Keys** section.
7. Save your changes.

Note, comments can also be allowed or disallowed for individual posts or pages.

page_title: How to Import WordPress Comments
heading_path: How Do I Export Comments from WordPress?
text:
### How Do I Export Comments from WordPress?

1. Navigate to **All Export › New Export**.
2. Choose **Comments** as the export post type.
3. Set any desired filters.
4. Select the export columns using the **Drag & Drop** screen.
5. Run the export and download the export file.

page_title: How to Import WordPress Comments
heading_path: How Do I Export All Comments from WordPress?
text:
### How Do I Export All Comments from WordPress?

1. Go to **All Export › New Export**.
2. Select **Comments** as the export type.
3. Do NOT set any export filters. This ensures that you will export all comments.
4. Choose the export columns.
5. Run the export and download the export file.

To learn more, see [How to Export WordPress Comments](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-export-wordpress-comments/).

page_title: How to Import WordPress Comments
heading_path: How Do I Bulk Edit My Existing Comments?
text:
### How Do I Bulk Edit My Existing Comments?

1. Create an export for the existing comments.
2. Download the export file.
3. Edit the export file in your favorite spreadsheet.
4. Import the modified file using the **Import with WP All Import** option.
5. Verify that the changes were correctly applied.

This requires you to have both WP All Import and WP All Export. Learn more about bulk editing data here: [How to Bulk Edit WooCommerce and WordPress Data](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/bulk-edit-woocommerce-wordpress-data/).

page_title: How to Import WordPress Comments
heading_path: How Do I Migrate Comments From One Site to Another?
text:
### How Do I Migrate Comments From One Site to Another?

1. Create an export with the desired comments.
2. Download the export bundle file, which includes import instructions.
3. On the destination site, go to **All Import › New Import** and upload the bundle file.
4. Run the import.
5. Verify that the comments were migrated as expected.

For this to work, the posts should match by title, as that's what is used in the bundle file. Learn more about this process here: [How to Migrate WooCommerce and WordPress Data](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-migrate-woocommerce-and-wordpress-data/).

heading_path: How Do I Export WooCommerce Products with Their Product Tags?
text:
### How Do I Export WooCommerce Products with Their Product Tags?

To export products with their product tags, create a new WooCommerce product export. When choosing your export columns, include the **Available Data > Taxonomies > Product tags** field.

page_title: How Do I Export Product Tags from WooCommerce?
heading_path: How Do I Export WooCommerce Products for a Specific Tag?
text:
### How Do I Export WooCommerce Products for a Specific Tag?

To export WooCommerce products for a specific product tag, run a standard export for WooCommerce products but create a filter where **Element** = **Product tags**, **Rule** = **In Product Tag**, and **Value** = the name of the product tag that you wish to export.

To learn more, see [How to Export WooCommerce Products to CSV, Excel, and XML](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-export-woocommerce-products-to-csv-or-xml/).

page_title: How Do I Automatically Import Products into WooCommerce?
heading_path: How Do I Automatically Import Products into WooCommerce?
text: To automatically import products into WooCommerce, you must first create a product import that uses a URL or FTP/SFTP site as its data source. You can then schedule the import via cron jobs on your server or by using our Automatic Scheduling service.

page_title: How Do I Automatically Import Products into WooCommerce?
heading_path: Steps to Automatically Import WooCommerce Products
text:
## Steps to Automatically Import WooCommerce Products

1. Create a WooCommerce product import as per [these instructions](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-import-woocommerce-products-csv-xml/). Make sure to select **Download a file > From URL** or **Download a file > From FTP/SFPT** as your import data source.
2. Run the import manually to verify that it works (the last thing you want to do is schedule an erroneous import).
3. Navigate to **All Import > Manage Imports** in the WordPress main menu.
4. Locate your import and click **Scheduling Options** on the right-hand side.
5. Configure the import schedule and save that configuration.

page_title: How Do I Automatically Import Products into WooCommerce?
heading_path: Understanding Your Scheduling Options
text:
## Understanding Your Scheduling Options

WP All Import offers two methods of scheduling:

- Automatic Scheduling Service
- Manual Scheduling

Of these, the Automatic Scheduling Service is by far the most convenient. For a small monthly fee, you can schedule your imports directly through this interface:

![Automatically Import Products WooCommerce Scheduling](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Automatically-Import-Products-WooCommerce-Scheduling-1024x729.jpg)

Schedules can be set on a daily, weekly, or monthly basis.

You can also schedule your imports manually by creating cron jobs on your server. For more information on this option, see [Scheduling Imports Using Cron Jobs](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/cron/).

![](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/plugins/custom-acf-blocks/blocks/docs-interstitial/img/octopus-logo.svg)

page_title: How to Import WooCommerce Data from CSV, Excel, XML, and Google Sheets
heading_path: Step 1: Create New Import for WooCommerce Data
text:
## Step 1: Create New Import for WooCommerce Data

Navigate to **All Import › New Import** in the main WordPress menu, select or upload your import file, then pick **WooCommerce Customers** from the drop-down list.

![Import WooCommerce Data Create a New Import](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Import-WooCommerce-Data-Create-a-New-Import-1024x659.png)

Click **Create Filters**.

To import from Google Sheets, you would provide the public sharing URL via the **Download a file** option.

page_title: How to Import WooCommerce Data from CSV, Excel, XML, and Google Sheets
heading_path: Step 2: Review the Import File
text:
## Step 2: Review the Import File

The **Review Import File** screen appears, where you can check to ensure that WP All Import has correctly interpreted your file.

![Import WooCommerce Data Review The Import File](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Import-WooCommerce-Data-Review-The-Import-File-1024x952.png)

You can also add filtering options in this step if you want to filter the customers to import, this is done under **Manage Filtering Options**. Once done, click **Continue to Setup Import** to proceed.

page_title: How to Import WooCommerce Data from CSV, Excel, XML, and Google Sheets
heading_path: Step 3: Map Incoming Data Elements to Target Fields
text:
## Step 3: Map Incoming Data Elements to Target Fields

You will now see the **Drag & Drop** screen, where you can map the incoming data elements to their WooCommerce customer fields. Simply drag & drop the data from the panel on the right to the correct fields on the left.

The first section is **Customer Account Info**, which contains the main customer information:

![Import WooCommerce Data Customer Account Info](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Import-WooCommerce-Data-Customer-Account-Info-1024x680.jpg)

Further down, the **Billing & Shipping Info** and **Other Customer Info** panels let you map the rest of the customer information.

![Import WooCommerce Data Import Other Fields](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Import-WooCommerce-Data-Import-Other-Fields-1024x790.jpg)

After completing this process, click **Continue to Import Settings** at the bottom (not shown).

page_title: How to Import WooCommerce Data from CSV, Excel, XML, and Google Sheets
heading_path: Step 4: Configure Settings and Run the Import
text:
## Step 4: Configure Settings and Run the Import

Next up is the **Import Settings**screen, where you define options and settings for the import. First, click on **Auto-detect** to define a unique identifier for the import. Below this, you can define how the import behaves when it runs again.

![Import WooCommerce Data Configure Import Settings](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Import-WooCommerce-Data-Configure-Import-Settings--1024x889.png)

You also have the following panels near the bottom:

- **Email Notifications For Imported Customers**: Enable the option inside this panel to avoid sending the default WordPress email notification to imported customers.
- **Scheduling Options**: Lets you set up different ways to schedule the import.
- **Configure Advanced Settings**: Includes various import settings and options to configure, such as records per iteration or the friendly name for the import.

For this example, leave these panels with their default selections.

Click the **Continue** button to move to the next step. You'll then see the **Confirm & Run** screen, where you can review an **Import Summary**.

page_title: How to Import WooCommerce Data from CSV, Excel, XML, and Google Sheets
heading_path: Step 4: Configure Settings and Run the Import
text:
tton to move to the next step. You'll then see the **Confirm & Run** screen, where you can review an **Import Summary**.

![Import WooCommerce Data Confirm and Run Import](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Import-WooCommerce-Data-Confirm-and-Run-Import-1024x846.jpg)

If everything looks okay, click **Confirm & Run Import**. Once the import completes, the screen will update as follows:

![Import WooCommerce Data Import Complete](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Import-WooCommerce-Data-Import-Complete-1024x863.jpg)

To import other types of WooCommerce data, see the information in the next sections.

text: ## Import WooCommerce Data — Advanced Topics

page_title: How to Import WooCommerce Data from CSV, Excel, XML, and Google Sheets
heading_path: Keeping the Same IDs When Importing WooCommerce Data
text:
### Keeping the Same IDs When Importing WooCommerce Data

When importing WooCommerce data, our plugin will treat each record as new when inserting it into the database, meaning it will be assigned a new ID. However, you can modify this behavior using custom code ([see documentation](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/inline-php/)) and our API ([see documentation](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/action-reference/)). Here are the available snippets:

- WooCommerce Products or Orders: [Keep old IDs when migrating products](https://www.wpallimport.com/snippet/keep-old-ids-when-migrating-products).
- WooCommerce Customers: [Keep Old IDs When Migrating Users or Customers](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/code-snippets/#keep-old-ids-when-migrating-users-or-customers).
- WooCommerce Reviews: [Keep Old IDs When Migrating Comments](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/code-snippets/#keep-old-ids-when-migrating-comments).

page_title: How to Import WooCommerce Data from CSV, Excel, XML, and Google Sheets
heading_path: Importing Data in the Correct Order
text:
### Importing Data in the Correct Order

If you wish to import multiple types of WooCommerce data, make sure to import WooCommerce products and customers first, followed by coupons, reviews, and orders. If you adhere to this sequence, WP All Import will help you maintain the relationships between different data types even if you can't preserve the original IDs.

page_title: How to Import WooCommerce Data from CSV, Excel, XML, and Google Sheets
heading_path: How to Filter WooCommerce Import Data Successfully
text:
### How to Filter WooCommerce Import Data Successfully

Filters can be added in Step 2 using the **Manage Filtering Options** panel. These filters let you control which WooCommerce records get imported.

A filter is composed of an **Element** (from your import file), a **Rule** (to apply), and a **Value** to filter. For example, when importing WooCommerce customers, you can choose the country as the element to import customers from the US only.

Once the filter is added, click **Add Rule** and then click **Apply Filters To XPath** to apply the newly created filter. This is what the result looks like:

![Import WooCommerce Data Add Filtering Options](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Import-WooCommerce-Data-Add-Filtering-Options-1024x487.jpg)

page_title: How to Import WooCommerce Data from CSV, Excel, XML, and Google Sheets
heading_path: WooCommerce Data Not Imported Correctly
text:
### WooCommerce Data Not Imported Correctly

In some cases, you may encounter problems importing your WooCommerce data. For example, when importing WooCommerce variable products, there can be issues with adding the variations correctly. If this happens, double-check our documentation to ensure you're setting up everything as expected.

If you're unable to figure out the issue, try to replicate your import and any problems in our sandbox environment ([https://www.wpallimport.com/debug/](https://www.wpallimport.com/debug/)). Then, just contact our support team and include the sandbox site URL and a brief description of what's wrong. You can contact Support by emailing us at [support@wpallimport.com](mailto:support@wpallimport.com) or using this form: [/support](https://www.wpallimport.com/support/).

page_title: How to Import WooCommerce Data from CSV, Excel, XML, and Google Sheets
heading_path: Import WooCommerce Data — Frequently Asked Questions
text: ## Import WooCommerce Data — Frequently Asked Questions

page_title: How to Import WooCommerce Data from CSV, Excel, XML, and Google Sheets
heading_path: How Do I Import an XML File Into WooCommerce?
text:
### How Do I Import an XML File Into WooCommerce?

1. Create a new import via **All Import › New Import**.
2. Upload your XML file with your WooCommerce data.
3. Select the correct WooCommerce post type.
4. Edit the import template and drag and drop your import fields.
5. Run the import and verify your imported WooCommerce data.

The steps to import an XML file into WooCommerce are essentially the same as described in the guide above.

page_title: How to Import WooCommerce Data from CSV, Excel, XML, and Google Sheets
heading_path: How Do I Import a CSV Attribute into WooCommerce?
text:
### How Do I Import a CSV Attribute into WooCommerce?

1. Go to **All Import › New Import** and upload your CSV with attributes.
2. Choose **Taxonomies**from the drop-down list.
3. Locate your desired attribute and continue.
4. Drag and drop the import fields to map the term's information.
5. Run the import and verify the newly imported attribute.

We explain more about importing taxonomy data here: [How to Import WordPress Categories and Tags](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-import-wordpress-categories-and-tags/).

page_title: How to Import WooCommerce Data from CSV, Excel, XML, and Google Sheets
heading_path: How Do I Schedule WooCommerce Data Imports?
text:
### How Do I Schedule WooCommerce Data Imports?

Assuming that you obtain your WooCommerce data from a URL or from an S/FTP source, you can schedule the imports using our available options to automate them. The following methods are available:

- **Automatic Scheduling Service**. This is an optional and paid monthly subscription that allows you to schedule imports from within WP All Import's interface. See [Automatic Scheduling](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/schedule-wordpress-imports/).
- **Manual cron jobs**. These are configured via your web host in cPanel or Plesk. See [Manual Scheduling](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/cron/).

page_title: How to Import WooCommerce Data from CSV, Excel, XML, and Google Sheets
heading_path: What Add-Ons Do I Need to Import WooCommerce Customers?
text:
### What Add-Ons Do I Need to Import WooCommerce Customers?

To import WooCommerce customers, you need to have both the WooCommerce Import Add-On and the User Import Add-On active on your site, along with WP All Import.

page_title: How to Import WooCommerce Coupons
heading_path: Step 1: Create a New Import for WooCommerce Coupons
text:
## Step 1: Create a New Import for WooCommerce Coupons

Navigate to **All Import › New Import**, upload your import file, and select **WooCommerce Coupons** from the list of available post types.

![Import WooCommerce Coupons Create New Import](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Import-WooCommerce-Coupons-Create-New-Import-1024x674.png)

Click **Create Filters** to move to the next step.

page_title: How to Import WooCommerce Coupons
heading_path: Step 2: Check the Import File Data
text:
## Step 2: Check the Import File Data

You now see the **Review Import File** screen, which lets you check the coupon data to import. If you see any problem with the data, fix the import file and start the import process again.

![Import WooCommerce Coupons Check the Import File](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Import-WooCommerce-Coupons-Check-the-Import-File-1024x857.png)

Below is a section called **Manage Filtering Options** (not shown), where you can define which coupons to import using filters.

Once done with this step, click **Continue to Setup Import**.

page_title: How to Import WooCommerce Coupons
heading_path: Step 3: Map the Incoming Data Elements to the Coupon Fields
text:
## Step 3: Map the Incoming Data Elements to the Coupon Fields

The **Drag & Drop** interface allows you to map the data elements from your import file into the relevant WooCommerce coupon fields. Just drag the data elements from the right panel and drop them on their corresponding field on the left.

page_title: How to Import WooCommerce Coupons
heading_path: Title & Content Section
text:
### Title & Content Section

First, map the title and the content. In this case, the title is the coupon code itself, and the content is the coupon's description:

![Import WooCommerce Coupons Drag and Drop](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Import-WooCommerce-Coupons-Drag-and-Drop-1024x750.jpg)

page_title: How to Import WooCommerce Coupons
heading_path: Custom Fields Section
text:
### Custom Fields Section

You need to import all other WooCommerce coupon information into the **Custom Fields** section. Here's a list of each custom field, what it's for, and what data it expects:

- **discount_type**: This is the type of discount to import. Accepted values: 
```
percent
```

, 
```
fixed_cart
```

, 
```
fixed_product
```

.
- **coupon_amount**: The value of the coupon. Accepts a numeric value.
- **expiry_date**: The date when the coupon will expire. Accepted format: YYYY-MM-DD. The coupon will expire at 00:00:00 on this date.
- **free_shipping**: Whether your coupon grants free shipping or not. Accepted values: 
```
yes
```

 or 
```
no
```

.
- **exclude_sale_items**: Enable this if the coupon should not apply to items on sale. Accepted values: 
```
yes
```

 or 
```
no
```

.
- **individual_use**: Enable this if the coupon cannot be used in conjunction with other coupons. Accepted values: 
```
yes
```

 or 
```
no
```

.
- **usage_limit**: Defines how many times this coupon can be used before it's void. Accepts numeric values. Input 
```
0
```

page_title: How to Import WooCommerce Coupons
heading_path: Custom Fields Section
text:
usage_limit**: Defines how many times this coupon can be used before it's void. Accepts numeric values. Input 
```
0
```

 for unlimited.
- **usage_limit_per_user**: Defines how many times this coupon can be used by an individual user. Accepts numeric values. Input 
```
0
```

 for unlimited.
- **limit_usage_to_x_items**: Defines the maximum number of individual items this coupon can apply to when using product discounts. Accepts numeric values. Input 
```
0
```

 for unlimited.
- **product_ids**: IDs of products that the coupon will apply to or that need to be in the cart in order for the "Fixed cart discount" to be applied. Accepts a comma-separated list of product IDs.
- **exclude_product_ids**: IDs of products that the coupon will not be applied to or that cannot be in the cart in order for the "Fixed cart discount" to be applied. Accepts a comma-separated list of product IDs.
- **product_categories**: A serialized array with the ID of product categories that the coupon will be applied to or that need to be in the cart in order for the "Fixed cart discount" to be applied. Expects a serialized array with the product category IDs.
- **exclude_product_categories**: A serialized ar

page_title: How to Import WooCommerce Coupons
heading_path: Custom Fields Section
text:
be applied. Expects a serialized array with the product category IDs.
- **exclude_product_categories**: A serialized array with the ID of product categories that the coupon will not be applied to or that cannot be in the cart in order for the "Fixed cart discount" to be applied. Expects a serialized array with the product category IDs.
- **minimum_amount**: Minimum spend amount allowed to use the coupon.
- **maximum_amount**: Maximum spend amount allowed when using the coupon.
- **customer_email**: A serialized array with a list of allowed billing emails to check against when an order is placed. You can use an asterisk (*) to match parts of an email. For example, "*@hotmail.com" would match all Hotmail addresses. Expects a serialized array with those emails.

![Import WooCommerce Coupons Custom Fields Required](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Import-WooCommerce-Coupons-Custom-Fields-Required-1024x767.jpg)

There are other import sections available, but these can be left alone for this example.

Once you've finished mapping the import template, click the **Continue to Import Settings** button at the bottom (not shown).

page_title: How to Import WooCommerce Coupons
heading_path: Step 4: Configure the Import Settings
text:
## Step 4: Configure the Import Settings

You now see the **Import Settings** screen. Here, you can configure many import settings. First, click **Auto-detect** to generate a unique identifier for each imported WooCommerce coupon. This identifier is used internally by WP All Import to keep track of the imported coupons.

![Import WooCommerce Coupons Define Import Settings](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Import-WooCommerce-Coupons-Define-Import-Settings-1024x769.png)

You can also change how the import process will behave when this import process runs again, including whether to create, update, or delete records.

Near the bottom, you will find panels to set up **Scheduling Options** and **Configure Advanced Settings**, though you can ignore these for now.

Click **Continue** at the bottom.

page_title: How to Import WooCommerce Coupons
heading_path: Step 5: Run the Import and Check The Coupons
text:
## Step 5: Run the Import and Check The Coupons

The **Confirm & Run** screen now appears. This screen lets you review everything the import will do before you run it:

![Import WooCommerce Caoupons Import Summary](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Import-WooCommerce-Coupons-Import-Summary-1024x845.jpg)

If it looks correct, click **Confirm & Run Import**. When the import finishes, you'll see the **Import Complete!** screen:

![Import WooCommerce Coupons Import Complete](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Import-WooCommerce-Coupons-Import-Complete-1024x866.jpg)

You can now go to **Marketing › Coupons** to review and confirm that all the coupons were imported correctly.

![](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/plugins/custom-acf-blocks/blocks/docs-interstitial/img/octopus-logo.svg)

page_title: How to Import WooCommerce Coupons
heading_path: Use Filters To Control Which Coupons To Import
text:
### Use Filters To Control Which Coupons To Import

Using the **Manage Filtering Options** section of the **Review Import File** screen, you can add import filters when setting up the import process. These filters let you control which coupons to import based on your import data and fields.

A filter needs an **Element**, a **Rule** to apply (such as equals), and a **Value** to filter. In this example, our **Element** is **discounttype**, our rule is **equals**, and our value is "fixed_cart".

After adding the filter values, click on **Add Rule**, and then click on **Apply Filters To XPath** to apply the filter you just created. Here is what the screen will look after you complete these steps:

![Import WooCommerce Coupons Filters](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Import-WooCommerce-Coupons-Filters-1024x449.jpg)

If you want to modify the filter for an existing import, you would simply need to locate that import in **All Import  › Manage Imports**, go into Edit Template, and proceed to edit the filters under **Manage Filtering Options** (found at the top of the screen).

page_title: How to Import WooCommerce Coupons
heading_path: Import Coupons From A Feed URL Or FTP Source
text:
### Import Coupons From A Feed URL Or FTP Source

In Step 1, we uploaded an import file to import WooCommerce coupons. However, you can also use a feed URL with the **Download a file › From URL** option:

![Import WooCommerce Coupons Download from URL](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Import-WooCommerce-Coupons-Download-from-URL-1024x419.jpg)

Alternatively, you can provide the file from an FTP source by selecting **Download a file › From FTP/SFTP** and inputting your FTP/SFTP details there.

page_title: How to Import WooCommerce Coupons
heading_path: Schedule To Import WooCommerce Coupons Automatically
text:
### Schedule To Import WooCommerce Coupons Automatically

You can schedule your coupon import to run automatically using either of our available options:

- Automatic Scheduling Service: This optional and paid service lets you set up imports directly from WP All Import's interface. It costs USD 9 per month and allows you to set up unlimited schedules. You can learn more here: [Run WordPress Import on a Schedule](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/schedule-wordpress-imports/).
- Manual Scheduling: You can also set up manual cron jobs, which are included at no extra cost. These cron jobs can be set up in your server or host. You can learn more here: [Scheduling Imports Using Cron Jobs](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/cron/).

heading_path: How Do I Bulk Add Coupons in WooCommerce?
text:
### How Do I Bulk Add Coupons in WooCommerce?

To bulk add coupons in WooCommerce, just follow this tutorial. "Bulk add" and "import" mean the same thing when referencing WooCommerce coupons.

page_title: How to Import WooCommerce Coupons
heading_path: How Do I Export WooCommerce Coupons?
text:
### How Do I Export WooCommerce Coupons?

1. Go to **All Export › New Export**.
2. Choose **WooCommerce Coupons** as the post type to export.
3. Click **Customize Export File** and build your export template.
4. Run the export and download the exported coupons.

You can learn more about this process in the following documentation: [How to Export WooCommerce Coupons](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-export-woocommerce-coupons/).

page_title: How to Import WooCommerce Coupons
heading_path: How Do I Export Store Data From WooCommerce?
text:
### How Do I Export Store Data From WooCommerce?

1. Got to **All Export › New Export**.
2. Choose the WooCommerce data you want to export from the dropdown list.
3. Configure the export template and run the export.
4. Download the exported store data from your WooCommerce site.

You can learn more about the export process in depth here: [How to Export WordPress to CSV, Excel, and XML](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-export-wordpress-csv-excel-xml/).

page_title: How to Import WooCommerce Coupons
heading_path: What Add-Ons Do I Need to Import WooCommerce Coupons?
text:
### What Add-Ons Do I Need to Import WooCommerce Coupons?

To import WooCommerce coupons, you need to have the WooCommerce Import Add-On active on your site, along with WP All Import.

page_title: How Do I Export Product Categories in WooCommerce?
heading_path: How Do I Export Product Categories in WooCommerce?
text: To export product categories in WooCommerce, go to **All Export > New Export** in the WordPress main menu. Select **Taxonomies** as the export post type and **Product categories** as the subtype. When configuring your export layout, be sure to select the **Term** fields of interest.

page_title: How Do I Export Product Categories in WooCommerce?
heading_path: Steps to Export Product Categories in WooCommerce
text:
## Steps to Export Product Categories in WooCommerce

1. Navigate to **All Export > New Export**.
2. Select **Taxonomies** as the export post type, followed by **Product categories** as the subtype.
3. Choose your export columns in the**Drag & Drop** screen.
4. If you have created product category hierarchies (i.e., subcategories) that you want to export, make sure to include the **Parent ID**, **Parent Name**, and **Parent Slug** columns.
5. Configure your export settings.
6. Run the export and download your file.

![](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/plugins/custom-acf-blocks/blocks/docs-interstitial/img/octopus-logo.svg)

page_title: How Do I Export Product Categories in WooCommerce?
heading_path: How Do I Export WooCommerce Products with Their Product Categories?
text:
### How Do I Export WooCommerce Products with Their Product Categories?

To export products with their product categories, create a new export with a post type of **WooCommerce Products**. While selecting your export columns, just include the **Available Data > Taxonomies > Product categories** field.

For more information, see [How to Export WooCommerce Products to CSV, Excel, and XML](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-export-woocommerce-products-to-csv-or-xml/).

page_title: How Do I Export Product Categories in WooCommerce?
heading_path: How Do I Export WooCommerce Products for a Specific Product Category?
text:
### How Do I Export WooCommerce Products for a Specific Product Category?

To export WooCommerce products for a specific product category, run a standard export for WooCommerce products but set a filter where **Element** = **Product categories**, **Rule** = **In Product Cat**, and the **Value** = the name of the category you want to export.

To learn more, see [How to Export WooCommerce Products to CSV, Excel, and XML](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-export-woocommerce-products-to-csv-or-xml/).

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Coupons
heading_path: Step 1: Create a New WooCommerce Coupon Export
text:
## Step 1: Create a New WooCommerce Coupon Export

Navigate to **All Export › New Export** and select **WooCommerce Coupons** as the export post type.

![Export WooCommerce Coupons Create New Export](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Export-WooCommerce-Coupons-Create-New-Export-1024x754.jpg)

WP All Export will display the number of coupons to export. You can also **Add Filtering Options** in this part.

Click **Customize Export File** to continue.

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Coupons
heading_path: Step 2: Drag and Drop the Export Columns
text:
## Step 2: Drag and Drop the Export Columns

You now see the **Drag & Drop**interface, where you can build your export file. To add columns to your export, drag them from the **Available Data** section on the right to the column selection area on the left:

![Export WooCommerce Coupons Drag and Drop Section](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Export-WooCommerce-Coupons-Drag-and-Drop-Section-1024x785.jpg)

Basic coupon data is in **Available Data › Standard**:

- Coupon ID: The coupon ID, assigned by WordPress.
- Coupon Code: The coupon code itself, used by users to receive the discount.
- Coupon Description: A description of the coupon, written by the creator of the coupon.
- Discount Type: One of several preset values. Percentage discounts are exported as 
```
percent
```

, fixed cart discounts as 
```
fixed_cart
```

, and discounts on specific products as 
```
fixed_product
```

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Coupons
heading_path: Step 2: Drag and Drop the Export Columns
text:
rcent
```

, fixed cart discounts as 
```
fixed_cart
```

, and discounts on specific products as 
```
fixed_product
```

.
- Coupon Amount: The amount to be discounted. Based on the discount type, this is either a percentage or a fixed amount.
- Expiry Date: Date when the coupon expires and is no longer available, in y-m-d format.
- Free Shipping: Whether this coupon grants free shipping or not, exports either 
```
yes
```

 or 
```
no
```

.
- Exclude Sale Items: Whether this coupon works on sale items or not, exports either 
```
yes
```

 or 
```
no
```

.

#### Other WooCommerce Coupon Data

Less used coupon data is in **Available Data › Other**. For example, data on who created the coupon, along with the following fields:

- usage_limit: The total number of times the coupon can be used.
- usage_limit_per_user: The number of times each customer can use the coupon.
- limit_usage_to_x_items: The maximum number of items allowed in the cart when using the coupon.
- usage_count: The number of times the coupon has been used.
- minimum_amount: Minimum cart amount required to apply the coupon.
- maximum_amount: Maximum cart amount allowed to apply the coupon.

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Coupons

- maximum_amount: Maximum cart amount allowed to apply the coupon.

![Export WooCommerce Coupons Other Section and Continue](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Export-WooCommerce-Coupons-Other-Section-and-Continue-1024x825.jpg)

Once you've selected all your export columns, click **Continue**.

text:
## Step 3: Configure the Export Settings and Run The Export

The **Export Settings** screen appears, where you can configure your export options. There are two panels available, **Scheduling Options** and **Advanced Options**, but you can leave them with their default settings for now.

![Export WooCommerce Coupons Settings](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Export-WooCommerce-Coupons-Settings-1024x849.jpg)

Click **Confirm & Run Export** or **Save & Run Export** to run the export.

Once the export finishes, you'll see the **Export Complete** screen. Here, you can **Click to Download** the exported WooCommerce coupons, as well as access other options within WP All Export.

![Export WooCommerce Coupons Export Complete](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Export-WooCommerce-Coupons-Export-Complete-1024x949.jpg)

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Coupons
heading_path: Send Exported Coupons Via Email
text:
### Send Exported Coupons Via Email

You can send your newly exported WooCommerce coupons via email using our Zapier integration, either with their native Email integration or using other third-party services such as Gmail. Learn more in [Send Exported WordPress Data via Email using Zapier](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/send-exported-wordpress-data-via-email-using-zapier/).

You can also send the exported WooCommerce coupons via email using custom code ([see documentation](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/inline-php/))  that uses our API ([see documentation](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/action-reference/)). Here's a working example snippet: [Send Export File via Email](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/code-snippets/#send-export-file-via-email).

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Coupons
heading_path: Filter the Coupons to Export
text:
### Filter the Coupons to Export

Our plugin allows you to filter your WooCommerce coupon exports. For example, there are three different types of coupons available: Percentage discount (percent), Fixed cart discount (fixed_cart), and Fixed product discount (fixed_product). If you want to export only WooCommerce coupons with a coupon type of "percent", create this filter:

![Export WooCommerce Coupons Filter What to Export](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Export-WooCommerce-Coupons-Filter-What-to-Export-1024x323.jpg)

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Coupons
heading_path: Schedule WooCommerce Coupons Export
text:
### Schedule WooCommerce Coupons Export

When you export WooCommerce coupons, you can automate that export to run automatically using either of our available options:

1. Automatic Scheduling Service. This is an optional and paid service that allows you to set up exports directly within our plugin. It provides an interface to let you easily define when to run your scheduled export and costs USD 9 per month. Learn more and subscribe here: [Run WordPress Export on a Schedule](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/run-wordpress-export-on-a-schedule/).
2. Manual Scheduling. This option is included without any extra costs and lets you schedule your export to run automatically using cron jobs. These cron jobs can be defined in your server, i.e., with cPanel or Plesk. Learn more here: [Schedule WordPress Exports Using Cron Jobs](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/scheduling-exports-using-cron-jobs/).

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Coupons
heading_path: Export WooCommerce Coupons — Frequently Asked Questions
text: ## Export WooCommerce Coupons — Frequently Asked Questions

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Coupons
heading_path: How Do I Bulk Edit WooCommerce Coupons?
text:
### How Do I Bulk Edit WooCommerce Coupons?

1. Export your WooCommerce coupons.
2. Edit the export file in your favorite spreadsheet.
3. Go to **All Export › Manage Exports** and search for your coupons export.
4. Use the **Import with WP All Import** option to upload your modified file.
5. Complete the import process.

You can learn more about the bulk edit process here: [How to Bulk Edit WooCommerce and WordPress Data](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/bulk-edit-woocommerce-wordpress-data/).

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Coupons
heading_path: How Do I Migrate Coupons Across Sites?
text:
### How Do I Migrate Coupons Across Sites?

1. In the source site, go to **All Export › New Export**.
2. Select**WooCommerce Coupons** and choose to**Migrate Coupons**.
3. Run the export and download the exported **Bundle** file.
4. In the destination site, go to **All Import › New Import**.
5. Upload the import file and complete the import.

You can learn more about the whole migration process here: [How to Migrate WooCommerce and WordPress Data](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-migrate-woocommerce-and-wordpress-data/).

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Coupons
heading_path: How Do I Bulk Upload Coupon Codes in WooCommerce?
text:
### How Do I Bulk Upload Coupon Codes in WooCommerce?

1. Create an import file with all WooCommerce coupons to upload.
2. Navigate to **All Import › New Import** and upload that file.
3. Choose **WooCommerce Coupons** as your import type.
4. Map the incoming data elements to the appropriate coupon data fields.
5. Run the import and check the imported data.

For more information, see [How to Import WooCommerce Coupons](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-import-woocommerce-coupons/).

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Coupons
heading_path: What Order Should I Use When Importing or Migrating WooCommerce Data?
text:
### What Order Should I Use When Importing or Migrating WooCommerce Data?

When you import or migrate WooCommerce data, keep the following order while importing to ensure that all data is imported correctly:

1. WooCommerce Products
2. WooCommerce Customers
3. WooCommerce Coupons
4. WooCommerce Orders
5. WooCommerce Reviews

If you're not importing any specific WooCommerce data from that list, you can simply ignore it and move on to the next one.

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Coupons
heading_path: Where Are WooCommerce Coupons Stored?
text:
### Where Are WooCommerce Coupons Stored?

WooCommerce Coupons are stored in your WordPress site's database, specifically in the wp_post table, and their specific details in the wp_postmeta table. Our plugin is able to read your database and obtain the data from this table to allow you to export WooCommerce coupons easily.

text:

To export WooCommerce coupons, you need to have the WooCommerce Export Add-On active on your site, along with WP All Export.

text:
## Step 1: Create a New Product Import

text:
## Step 2: Review the Import File

Here, you'll see the products and images that will be imported. You can choose a different node when importing XML or change the delimiter with CSV files.

Near the end of the page, you can add filtering options using the **Manage Filtering Options** section (not shown). Once done, click on **Continue to Setup Import**.

heading_path: Step 3: Map the Incoming Data Elements to the Product Fields
text:
## Step 3: Map the Incoming Data Elements to the Product Fields

You'll now see the **Drag & Drop** interface, where you can map the import data to WooCommerce fields. To do so, drag and drop the import data elements from the right panel to their appropriate fields in the left section.

page_title: Import WooCommerce Products with Images
heading_path: Title & Description Section
text:
### Title & Description Section

Start by mapping the title and description for your WooCommerce products.

![](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Import-Products-with-Images-Title-and-Description-1024x660.jpg)

Next, expand the **WooCommerce Add-On** section.

page_title: Import WooCommerce Products with Images
heading_path: WooCommerce Add-On Section
text:
### WooCommerce Add-On Section

This section lets you define everything about the products you're importing. You first define the **Product Type**, though it can be left with its default selection.

![Import Products with Images WooCommerce Add On Section](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Import-Products-with-Images-WooCommerce-Add-On-Section-1024x677.jpg)

To learn in-depth how to set up this section, see [WooCommerce Add-On Section](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-import-woocommerce-products-csv-xml/#wooco-addon).

page_title: Import WooCommerce Products with Images
heading_path: Images Section
text:
### Images Section

This is where you define how to import your products with images. Here's how this section looks:

![Import Products with Images See Images Section](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Import-Products-with-Images-See-Images-Section-1024x681.jpg)

page_title: Import WooCommerce Products with Images
heading_path: Download images hosted elsewhere
text:
### Download images hosted elsewhere

This option is selected by default. It lets you input an image URL, using either the HTTP or HTTPS protocol, to download and add to both your Media Library and your imported product. You can input one URL per line or separate multiple URLs with commas.

page_title: Import WooCommerce Products with Images
heading_path: Use images currently in Media Library
text:
### Use images currently in Media Library

This option lets you import image filenames to match with existing images in your Media Library.

page_title: Import WooCommerce Products with Images
heading_path: Use images currently uploaded in wp-content/uploads/wpallimport/files/
text:
### Use images currently uploaded in wp-content/uploads/wpallimport/files/

This is the last option. It also lets you import image filenames to match with images, but you'll match the names found in the /wp-content/uploads/wpallimport/files/ folder. You can also use subfolders within this folder to keep images ordered.

![](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/plugins/custom-acf-blocks/blocks/docs-interstitial/img/octopus-logo.svg)

heading_path: Images Options
text: ### Images Options These options are available in the **Images** section and can be left alone if you don't want to modify the default behavior: - Search through the Media Library for existing images before importing new images - Keep images currently in Media Library - Scan through post content and import images wrapped in tags - Set the first image to the Featured Image (_thumbnail_id) - If no images are downloaded successfully, create entry as Draft. If you're not certain what an option does, check its help tooltip.

heading_path: SEO & Advanced Options
text:
### SEO & Advanced Options

This is found near the end of the **Images** section and lets you define the metadata for the imported images. You can also change the image filename and extension here:

![Import Products with Images SEO and Advanced Options](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Import-Products-with-Images-SEO-and-Advanced-Options-1024x422.jpg)

heading_path: Other Sections
text:
### Other Sections

There are other sections to fill in when setting up your import for products with images, such as **Custom Fields**, **Other Product Options**, etc. However, we won't cover them here.

page_title: Import WooCommerce Products with Images
heading_path: Preview an Import Process
text:
### Preview an Import Process

After you map all of your fields, you can preview your import process by clicking the **Preview** button at the bottom.

![Preview Import Process](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Preview-Import-Process-1024x562.jpg)

The **Preview** screen will mimic how records will be imported and display those records as they would appear in the backend, or in the frontend.

If the records in the preview don't look correct, continue updating the import settings and mappings until they are. Once you're happy with the way the previewed records look, you can proceed to run the import process.

Learn more about previewing an import here: [How to Preview An Import Before Running It](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-preview-an-import/).

Once all data elements have been mapped, click **Continue to Import Settings** at the bottom (not shown).

page_title: Import WooCommerce Products with Images
heading_path: Step 4: Configure the Import Settings
text:
## Step 4: Configure the Import Settings

The **Import Settings** screen appears now, where you can modify the import configuration. First, click on **Auto-detect** to generate a unique identifier automatically.

![Import Products with Images Define Import Settings](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Import-Products-with-Images-Define-Import-Settings-1024x890.png)

In the section below that, you can define how the import behaves when it runs again, i.e., to create, update, or delete existing records.

The next panels are **Scheduling Options** and **Configure Advanced Settings**. However, these can be left alone for now.

To proceed, click **Continue**.

page_title: Import WooCommerce Products with Images
heading_path: Step 5: Run the Import and Verify the Imported Data
text:
## Step 5: Run the Import and Verify the Imported Data

WP All Import will show the **Confirm & Run** screen next, which lets you review an import summary:

![Import Products with Images Confirm and Run](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Import-Products-with-Images-Confirm-and-Run-1024x760.jpg)

Once you confirm everything is okay, click **Confirm & Run Import** to run the import. The following notice will appear when the import completes:

![Import Products with Images Import Complete](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Import-Products-with-Images-Import-Complete-1024x788.jpg)

You should then navigate to **Products › All Products** to check that your import worked as expected:

![Import Products with Images All Products](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Import-Products-with-Images-All-Products-1024x658.jpg)

That's it!

![](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/plugins/custom-acf-blocks/blocks/docs-interstitial/img/octopus-logo.svg)

    - Product variations & attributes
- Price & stock
- Images & galleries

page_title: Import WooCommerce Products with Images
heading_path: Import Products With Images – Advanced Topics
text: ## Import Products With Images – Advanced Topics

page_title: Import WooCommerce Products with Images
heading_path: Import Extra Images to Product Variations
text:
### Import Extra Images to Product Variations

Out of the box, variable products only allow one image per variation. However, there are plugins that modify this behavior. For example, when either of the following is installed:

- [WooCommerce Additional Variation Images](https://woocommerce.com/products/woocommerce-additional-variation-images/)
- [Additional Variation Images Gallery for WooCommerce](https://wordpress.org/plugins/woo-variation-gallery/)

To accommodate this situation, simply enable the **Import additional variation images** option:

![Import Products with Images Additional Variation Images](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Import-Products-with-Images-Additional-Variation-Images-1024x757.jpg)

If you use a different plugin, you'll need to write some code that uses our [pmxi_gallery_image](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/action-reference/#pmxi_gallery_image) hook to import the additional variation images into it.

page_title: Import WooCommerce Products with Images
heading_path: Troubleshoot Import Product Images
text:
### Troubleshoot Import Product Images

If the product images don't import correctly, you can troubleshoot by doing the following:

- Check the History Log to understand the error and act accordingly.
- When importing from an URL, check that the image exists and can be downloaded programmatically.
- While editing the template, whatever option you choose to import images, use the **Preview** button to test that the images work as expected.

page_title: Import WooCommerce Products with Images
heading_path: Import Images From an FTP or SFTP Source
text:
### Import Images From an FTP or SFTP Source

You can import images stored in an FTP or SFTP source by using a custom code to fetch the images. For example, you can adapt the following code: [Workaround for Importing from FTP](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/code-snippets/#workaround-for-importing-from-ftp) to fetch the images from your FTP source:

```
[custom_file_download("http://example.com/full/path/to/image-file.jpg","jpg")]
```

Here is the usage:

![Import Products with Images From FTP](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Import-Products-with-Images-From-FTP-1024x356.jpg)

page_title: Import WooCommerce Products with Images
heading_path: Offload Images From WordPress to Other Services
text:
### Offload Images From WordPress to Other Services

Out of the box, all images will be automatically downloaded to WordPress, and it's not possible to avoid this behavior. That said, there are third-party plugins capable of offloading imported images to another service, such as an Amazon S3 bucket. In other words, after the image has been imported to WordPress, then it gets offloaded.

page_title: Import WooCommerce Products with Images
heading_path: Import Products With Images – Frequently Asked Questions
text: ## Import Products With Images – Frequently Asked Questions

page_title: Import WooCommerce Products with Images
heading_path: How Do I Import a Product Image into WooCommerce?
text:
### How Do I Import a Product Image into WooCommerce?

To import a product image into WooCommerce, follow the steps from this guide. You can import a single product with a single image, or you can import multiple products, each with multiple images.

page_title: Import WooCommerce Products with Images
heading_path: How Do I Import Product Images from CSV or Google Sheets to WooCommerce?
text:
### How Do I Import Product Images from CSV or Google Sheets to WooCommerce?

To import images from CSV or Google Sheets to WooCommerce, just specify the CSV file as your import data source. Our plugin will automatically recognize the file format and adjust its interfaces accordingly.

page_title: Import WooCommerce Products with Images
heading_path: How Do I Import Products with Multiple Images in WooCommerce?
text:
### How Do I Import Products with Multiple Images in WooCommerce?

To import products with multiple images, the **Images** section provides you with several options. For example, if an incoming data element contains multiple images per product, you can specify the character that our plugin should use to separate those images.

For more information, see [Variable Number Of Images Per Post](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/variable-number-of-images-per-post/).

page_title: Import WooCommerce Products with Images
heading_path: How Do I Bulk Upload Products with Images in WooCommerce?
text:
### How Do I Bulk Upload Products with Images in WooCommerce?

1. Go to **All Import › New Import** and upload your import file.
2. Choose **WooCommerce Products** from the dropdown list.
3. Map the incoming data elements to your product fields.
4. Be sure to map the product image fields.
5. Run the import and review your newly imported data.

To learn more about this process, please refer to the full guide above.

page_title: Import WooCommerce Products with Images
heading_path: How Do I Export All Products From WooCommerce with Images?
text:
### How Do I Export All Products From WooCommerce with Images?

1. Navigate to **All Export › New Export**.
2. Choose to export **WooCommerce Products**.
3. Set up your export columns and include your product images.
4. Run the export and download the export file.

Learn more about this process here: [Export WooCommerce Products with Images](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/export-woocommerce-products-with-images/).

page_title: Import WooCommerce Products with Images
heading_path: What Add-Ons Do I Need to Import WooCommerce Products?
text:
### What Add-Ons Do I Need to Import WooCommerce Products?

To import WooCommerce products, you need to have the WooCommerce Import Add-On active on your site, along with WP All Import.

page_title: How to Send Exported WordPress Data to Zapier
heading_path: How to Send Exported WordPress Data to Zapier
text: To send exported WordPress data to Zapier, create a Zap that triggers an action whenever the export runs, such as emailing the exported data to someone or sending it to Google Drive. Zapier helps you automate this process.

page_title: How to Send Exported WordPress Data to Zapier
heading_path: Table of Contents
text:
## Table of Contents

- [Step 1: Create and Customize Your Data Export](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-send-exported-wordpress-data-to-zapier/#create-export)
- [Step 2: Connect Zapier And WordPress](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-send-exported-wordpress-data-to-zapier/#connect-zapier)
- [Step 3: Create An Action in Zapier](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-send-exported-wordpress-data-to-zapier/#create-action)
- [Step 4: Publish The New Zap](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-send-exported-wordpress-data-to-zapier/#publish-new-zap)

**Advanced Topics**

- [Automatically Run WordPress Export](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-send-exported-wordpress-data-to-zapier/#schedules)
- [Connect Exported Data To Your Custom API](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-send-exported-wordpress-data-to-zapier/#connect-to-api)

page_title: How to Send Exported WordPress Data to Zapier
heading_path: Step 1: Create and Customize Your Data Export
text:
## Step 1: Create and Customize Your Data Export

Create your desired [custom WordPress export](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-export-wordpress-csv-xml/). Once your export is complete, you can send the export files to any app enabled in Zapier.

Note that the Zapier integration can only process your export file, not the data contained inside the file. If you want to process the data itself, you should review this guide: [Integrate Zapier with WooCommerce for Real-Time Exports](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/integrate-zapier-with-woocommerce-for-real-time-exports/).

page_title: How to Send Exported WordPress Data to Zapier
heading_path: Step 2: Connect Zapier And WordPress
text:
## Step 2: Connect Zapier And WordPress

Go to [Zapier.com](https://zapier.com/), create an account (or login), and make a new Zap by using the **+ Create Zap** button.

![Zapier WordPress Integration Create a Zap](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Zapier-WordPress-Integration-Create-a-Zap-1024x659.jpg)

page_title: How to Send Exported WordPress Data to Zapier
heading_path: Trigger App & Event
text:
### Trigger App & Event

First, select **WP All Export Pro** as the trigger app. The trigger is what initiates the connection between Zapier and WordPress.

![Zapier WordPress Select Trigger App](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Zapier-WordPress-Select-Trigger-App-1024x869.jpg)

After choosing the trigger app, select the trigger event next. Here, you select **New Export File**:

![Zapier WordPress Event New Export File](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Zapier-WordPress-Event-New-Export-File-1024x835.jpg)

When an export is successfully completed, WP All Export will send Zapier the export information, such as the export file’s URL, the post types exported, the number of records in the export file, etc.

page_title: How to Send Exported WordPress Data to Zapier
heading_path: Account
text:
### Account

Here you choose what account or site to connect with:

![Zapier WordPress Choose Account](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Zapier-WordPress-Choose-Account-1024x780.jpg)

If you haven't linked your WP All Export account yet, Zapier will ask you to sign in. To connect Zapier to your WordPress site, you need your site's URL and your API key, which can be found in **All Export › Settings**. Then, in Zapier, go to this screen by using the **+ Connect a new account** button.

![](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/Connect-Zapier-WP-All-Export-Pro-1024x731.png)

Once you've connected Zapier to WordPress, you select that site as the account:

![Zapier WordPress Site Account Chosen](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Zapier-WordPress-Site-Account-Chosen-1024x831.jpg)

page_title: How to Send Exported WordPress Data to Zapier
heading_path: Test
text:
### Test

This is where you test what you've done so far by making sure that the connection between Zapier and WordPress is correct. The latest export on your site will be retrieved:

![Zapier WordPress Test The Trigger](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Zapier-WordPress-Test-The-Trigger-1024x887.jpg)

Once you've verified the trigger, you can move on to the action.

page_title: How to Send Exported WordPress Data to Zapier
heading_path: Step 3: Create An Action in Zapier
text:
## Step 3: Create An Action in Zapier

The action will be applied to the exported data. For example, let's say that you want to send the exported WooCommerce orders as a report via email.

To do that, connect Zapier and Gmail, then use an action to send a new email with the exported file.

While the specific fields may change when using other apps as the action, these same instructions apply.

Keep in mind that you can add multiple actions or workflows in this part. For example, you can add a filter so that this Zap only runs when the Export ID matches a certain value:

![Zapier WordPress Filter Correct Export ID](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Zapier-WordPress-Filter-Correct-Export-ID-1024x885.jpg)

If no filter is chosen here, the Zap will run for the latest export that it finds regardless of its Export ID.

page_title: How to Send Exported WordPress Data to Zapier
heading_path: App
text:
### App

Following our example, we choose Gmail as our action app:

![Zapier WordPress Select Action App](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Zapier-WordPress-Select-Action-App-1024x881.jpg)

page_title: How to Send Exported WordPress Data to Zapier
heading_path: Event
text:
### Event

For this event, we select **Send Email**. This is performed when the Zap executes.

![Zapier WordPress Send Email in Gmail](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Zapier-WordPress-Send-Email-in-Gmail-1024x823.jpg)

page_title: How to Send Exported WordPress Data to Zapier
heading_path: Account
text:
### Account

Next, choose the Gmail account to use:

![Zapier WordPress Choose Gmail Account](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Zapier-WordPress-Choose-Gmail-Account-1024x828.jpg)

page_title: How to Send Exported WordPress Data to Zapier
heading_path: Action
text:
### Action

Here, we define the details for the new email that will be generated upon every export, such as selecting who to send the email to, the subject, whether to include a body or content, etc.:

![Zapier WordPress Send Email Action](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Zapier-WordPress-Send-Email-Action-1024x888.jpg)

Finally, choose the export file to include as an attachment.

![Zapier WordPress Send Email Attach Export](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Zapier-WordPress-Send-Email-Attach-Export-1024x398.jpg)

Once you've added your required fields, you can proceed to the test.

page_title: How to Send Exported WordPress Data to Zapier
heading_path: Test
text:
### Test

Here, Zapier will ask you to test the trigger with a recent export.

![Zapier WordPress Test Gmail](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Zapier-WordPress-Test-Gmail-1024x887.jpg)

Run the test to confirm that your email is working as expected.

You can chain other apps in this step, such as adding the exported orders data to a Google Drive. You're almost done connecting Zapier and WordPress.

page_title: How to Send Exported WordPress Data to Zapier
heading_path: Step 4: Publish The New Zap
text:
## Step 4: Publish The New Zap

Once you've configured the Zap as needed, click the **Publish Zap** button (see image above), and confirm that it's working as expected by performing a test export.

Remember that every time the export runs, the trigger will execute and perform the action as configured inside Zapier. Here's the full list of apps supported by Zapier: [http://zapier.com/zapbook/wp-all-export-pro/](http://zapier.com/zapbook/wp-all-export-pro/).

You can learn more about using Zapier: [https://zapier.com/learn/getting-started-guide/what-is-zapier/](https://zapier.com/learn/getting-started-guide/what-is-zapier/).

![](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/plugins/custom-acf-blocks/blocks/docs-interstitial/img/octopus-logo.svg)

heading_path: Export WordPress Data to any CSV, XML, or Excel
text:

    - Any theme or plugin

page_title: How to Send Exported WordPress Data to Zapier
heading_path: Advanced Topics
text: ## Advanced Topics

page_title: How to Send Exported WordPress Data to Zapier
heading_path: Automatically Run WordPress Export
text:
### Automatically Run WordPress Export

You can make your Zapier to WordPress integration run automatically by scheduling your export to run at a specific time, such as daily, weekly, or monthly. For this, you can use either:

- Automatic Scheduling: This can be set up directly from within WP All Export's interface. Simply go to the **Scheduling Options** and define your schedules. This service costs USD 9/month. Learn more here: [Run WordPress Export on a Schedule](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/run-wordpress-export-on-a-schedule/).
- Manual Scheduling: You can set up manual cron jobs via cPanel or Plesk. Using both a "trigger" and a "processing" cron job, you can automate your export. Learn more here: [Schedule WordPress Exports Using Cron Jobs](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/scheduling-exports-using-cron-jobs/).

page_title: How to Send Exported WordPress Data to Zapier
heading_path: Connect Exported Data To Your Custom API
text:
### Connect Exported Data To Your Custom API

You can use custom code ([see documentation](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/custom-wordpress-export-php/)) along with our API ([see documentation](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/action-reference/)) to send your exported data in whatever way you need. This uses just WordPress without Zapier. For example, you may have a PHP script to POST your data to an API in a specific format. After the export runs, you can obtain the exported file using the [pmxe_after_export](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/action-reference/#pmxe_after_export) filter. Specifically, you can get the file path for the exported file in 
```
$exportObj->options["current_filepath"]
```

.

page_title: How to Send Exported WordPress Data to Zapier
heading_path: Frequently Asked Questions
text: ## Frequently Asked Questions

page_title: How to Send Exported WordPress Data to Zapier
heading_path: Can Zapier Integrate With WordPress?
text:
### Can Zapier Integrate With WordPress?

Yes, and it can be enhanced by using WP All Export Pro, which allows you to send your exported data to thousands of the most popular apps available via Zapier. This gives you greater control in automating and controlling your exports and reports without the need for code. WP All Export Pro is the best way to connect Zapier and WordPress.

page_title: How to Send Exported WordPress Data to Zapier
heading_path: Can I Use Zapier With WP All Export Free?
text:
### Can I Use Zapier With WP All Export Free?

Unfortunately, no. You need WP All Export Pro to access the Zapier integration and connect your Zapier to WordPress. If you want to try out this integration, you can do so via our [sandbox environment](http://www.wpallimport.com/try/).
