page_title: How to Install and Update WP All Import Plugins
heading_path: How to Install and Update WP All Import Plugins
text: To install or update WP All Import, WP All Export, or any of our add-ons, you would use the regular WordPress interface. Obtain the installation file from our Customer Portal. Once done, go to **Plugins › Add New**, upload that file, and then activate the plugin afterward.

page_title: How to Install and Update WP All Import Plugins
heading_path: Step 1: Download Installation Files
text:
## Step 1: Download Installation Files

Download the latest version of the plugin or add-on that will be installed from the [Customer Portal](https://www.wpallimport.com/portal/).

You can obtain the installation files from two places in our Customer Portal:

1. After logging in, you will see your available purchases. Click on **View Details and Downloads**, next to your purchase, to download your plugin files.
2. Or, navigate to the **Downloads** section on the left menu. You can also use this link: [https://www.wpallimport.com/portal/downloads/](https://www.wpallimport.com/portal/downloads/).

![Downloads Customer Portal](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/Downloads-Customer-Portal-1024x759.jpg)

page_title: How to Install and Update WP All Import Plugins
heading_path: Step 2: Install The Downloaded File
text:
## Step 2: Install The Downloaded File

Here's how to upload WP All Import, WP All Export, or any add-on to your site:

1. Go to **Plugins › Add New**
2. Select **Upload Plugin**.
3. Drag and drop the .ZIP file to this area, or use the **Choose File** option to select it.
4. Once selected, click on **Install Now** and **Activate Plugin** on the next page.

![Add New Plugin How To](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/Add-New-Plugin-How-To-1024x657.png)

That's it! You can start importing or exporting right away.

If you have a free or old version already installed, deactivate and delete that version before taking the steps described above. Deleting an old or free version is safe and won't change your imports or existing settings. Those will remain the same.

page_title: How to Install and Update WP All Import Plugins
heading_path: Step 3: Enter License Key
text:
## Step 3: Enter License Key

To obtain access and download the latest available versions of **WP All Import** and/or **WP All Export**from your WordPress admin panel, you'll have to enter your license key.

- WP All Import: you can add its license key to the **WP All Import License Key** field in **All Import › Settings**, then click **Save License**.

- WP All Export: you can add its license key to the **WP All Export License Key** field in **All Export › Settings**, then click **Save License**.

You can obtain your License Keys by navigating to our [Customer Portal](https://www.wpallimport.com/portal/) and then clicking on **View Details and Downloads** next to your purchase.

License keys are only required for our core import/export plugins. All other add-ons can access the latest versions without a license key.

page_title: How to Install and Update WP All Import Plugins
heading_path: Advanced Topics and Troubleshooting
text: ## Advanced Topics and Troubleshooting

page_title: How to Install and Update WP All Import Plugins
heading_path: How to Update WP All Import, WP All Export, or add-ons
text:
### How to Update WP All Import, WP All Export, or add-ons

Once an update is available, you'll be able to click **Update Now** via the **Plugins › Installed Plugins** page. If for some reason, updating via WordPress is not possible, you can do it manually:

- While in **Plugins › Installed Plugins**, search for the old version of the plugin, deactivate and delete it.
- Once the old version is deleted, you can follow the flow described in Step 2 of this guide to upload the latest version.

As explained above, deleting an old or free version is safe and won't change your imports or existing settings, since those will remain unaffected.

page_title: How to Install and Update WP All Import Plugins
heading_path: Install or Update via FTP
text:
### Install or Update via FTP

If you cannot install the plugin normally, as described above, you can also do so via FTP. Here's what you need to do:

1. Extract the downloaded plugin ZIP file to a folder on your computer.
2. Upload the plugin folder to your FTP server inside the directory /
```
wp-content
```

/
```
plugins/
```

.
3. Navigate to **Plugins › Installed Plugins** on your WordPress site and activate the plugin

When updating the plugin via FTP, you can delete the old plugin version from the plugins folder and then upload the new one.

page_title: How to Install and Update WP All Import Plugins
heading_path: Upgrading From Free to Pro Version
text:
### Upgrading From Free to Pro Version

If you have **WP All Import** or **WP All Export** free and are looking to upgrade to the Pro version, you would simply need to delete the free version before installing the Pro version. Your import settings, templates, and history will remain untouched. To install the Pro version, see [Step 2](#install-process) from this guide.

page_title: How to Install and Update WP All Import Plugins
heading_path: Frequently Asked Questions
text: ## Frequently Asked Questions

page_title: How to Install and Update WP All Import Plugins
heading_path: What's The Automatic Scheduling License Key For?
text:
### What's The Automatic Scheduling License Key For?

The **Automatic Scheduling License Key** field found on the **Settings** page is used to enter your license key for our Automatic Scheduling service. This key is different than the License Key described in Step 3 of this guide, and has to be acquired separately via a monthly subscription.

To learn more, see [Run WordPress Import on a Schedule](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/schedule-wordpress-imports/).

page_title: How to Install and Update WP All Import Plugins
heading_path: My Developer Bought The Plugin, Can I Access Updates and Support?
text:
### My Developer Bought The Plugin, Can I Access Updates and Support?

Yes. But, you'll need to go through your developer (or whoever purchased the plugin) for updates and support. If you want to access the plugin files, updates, or support directly with us, you'd need to purchase your own license.

We explain more about our License here: [https://www.wpallimport.com/license/](https://www.wpallimport.com/license/).

page_title: How to Install and Update WP All Import Plugins
heading_path: Can I Download an Old Version?
text:
### Can I Download an Old Version?

We can only provide support for the latest stable versions available. That said, you can request access to download an older version by emailing us at support@wpallimport.com, or sending a [Support](https://www.wpallimport.com/support/) request via our website.

page_title: How to Bulk Edit WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: Step 1: Select the Type of Data to Bulk Edit
text:
## Step 1: Select the Type of Data to Bulk Edit

Go to **All Export › New Export** and select the post type that you wish to bulk edit.

![Bulk Edit WordPress Data New Export](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Bulk-Edit-WordPress-Data-New-Export-1024x859.jpg)

You can also add data filters in this step.

Once done, click **Customize Export File** to continue (not shown).

page_title: How to Bulk Edit WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: Step 2: Select the Data Columns to Export
text:
## Step 2: Select the Data Columns to Export

Next up is the **Drag & Drop** screen. All of the fields available for data export are shown in the **Available Data** section on the right. To include a field in the export, drag it from that section to the column selection area.

![Bulk Edit WordPress Data Drag and Drop](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Bulk-Edit-WordPress-Data-Drag-and-Drop-1024x766.jpg)

If you intend to bulk edit this data, you must include key identifying fields so that WP All Import can correctly apply your changes when you import them. Depending on the post type being exported, you'll see a message if you're missing any of these key fields.

Once you've added the fields that you want to bulk edit, click the **Continue** button (not shown).

page_title: How to Bulk Edit WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: Step 3: Confirm & Run the Export
text:
## Step 3: Confirm & Run the Export

You'll now see the **Export Settings** screen. Here, you can set **Advanced Options** for the export, such as whether to only export posts that have been modified since the last export. You can also schedule your export to run automatically. For this example, we'll leave these settings as they are.

![Bulk Edit WordPress Data Confirm and Run Export Process](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/Bulk-Edit-WordPress-Data-Confirm-and-Run-Export-Process-1024x860.png)

Click **Confirm & Run Export**.

page_title: How to Bulk Edit WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: Step 4: Download and Bulk Edit the Export File
text:
## Step 4: Download and Bulk Edit the Export File

Once the export completes, download the generated CSV file.

![Bulk Edit WordPress Data Download CSV](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Bulk-Edit-WordPress-Data-Download-CSV-1024x664.jpg)

Using the program of your choice (spreadsheets like Excel, Numbers, etc., make excellent editors), open the export file and make the required bulk edits. Don't remove any columns, or there may be missing data after the import runs.

![Bulk Edit WordPress Data Modify CSV File](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Bulk-Edit-WordPress-Data-Modify-CSV-File-1024x570.jpg)

Save the edited file using the same file type.

page_title: How to Bulk Edit WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: Step 5: Import the Modified Data to Apply the Changes
text:
## Step 5: Import the Modified Data to Apply the Changes

If you click the **Export, Edit, Import** tab in WP All Export's **Confirm & Run** screen, you will see an **Import with WP All Import** option near the bottom of the screen.

![Bulk Edit WordPress Data Import with WP All Import](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Bulk-Edit-WordPress-Data-Import-with-WP-All-Import-1024x641.jpg)

If you already closed that screen, you can find the same option in **All Export › Manage Exports**:

![Bulk Edit WordPress Data Manage Exports](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Bulk-Edit-WordPress-Data-Manage-Exports-1024x347.jpg)

To import your modified data, click this option. It will automatically configure the import settings based on the fields you set in your export.

After selecting this option, you'll be asked to provide the import file. Choose **Upload a file** and select the CSV file that you just modified.

![Bulk Edit WordPress Data Upload a File](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/Bulk-Edit-WordPress-Data-Upload-a-File--1024x447.png)

Your file will be uploaded. Then, click **Set Up Import** to continue the bulk edit process.

page_title: How to Bulk Edit WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: Step 5: Import the Modified Data to Apply the Changes
text:
load-a-File--1024x447.png)

Your file will be uploaded. Then, click **Set Up Import** to continue the bulk edit process.

![Bulk Edit WordPress Data Mapped Fields](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Bulk-Edit-WordPress-Data-Mapped-Fields-1024x648.jpg)

The data to be updated is already mapped to the appropriate import fields. You can examine what was mapped or just continue to the next step.

page_title: How to Bulk Edit WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: Step 6: Confirm & Run the Import, then Verify the Bulk Edit Changes
text:
## Step 6: Confirm & Run the Import, then Verify the Bulk Edit Changes

Next, you'll see the **Import Settings** screen. Here you can modify what data to update during the import, though there is seldom a need to change this. Click **Continue** at the bottom.

![Bulk Edit WordPress Data The Import Settings Screen](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/Bulk-Edit-WordPress-Data-The-Import-Settings-Screen-746x1024.jpg)

Finally, you'll arrive at the **Confirm & Run** screen (not shown), where you can review an **Import Summary** section. Check the import data and click on **Confirm & Run Import** button to run the import and finish the bulk edit process.

![Bulk Edit WordPress Data Import Complete](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Bulk-Edit-WordPress-Data-Import-Complete-1024x617.jpg)

Once the import is complete, you can check the edited records to confirm that the bulk edit changes were applied as expected.

heading_path: Bulk Edit WooCommerce and WordPress – Advanced Topics
text: ## Bulk Edit WooCommerce and WordPress – Advanced Topics

page_title: How to Bulk Edit WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: Filter Data to Selectively Bulk Edit
text:
### Filter Data to Selectively Bulk Edit

If you don't need to bulk edit all the records from your selected post type, you can use export filters to select which records to edit.

To do so, use **Add Filtering Options** when creating the new export and create your desired filter.

![Bulk Edit WordPress Data Add Filtering Options](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Bulk-Edit-WordPress-Data-Add-Filtering-Options-1024x491.jpg)

If your export already exists, you can add filters via **All Export › Manage Exports › Settings** before running it again.

Learn more about adding filters in: [Filter Exported WordPress Data](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/filter-exported-wordpress-data/).

page_title: How to Bulk Edit WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: Bulk Edit WordPress Data via PHP Code
text:
### Bulk Edit WordPress Data via PHP Code

The bulk edit process described in this document requires that you export the data, modify it externally, and then re-import it back into your site.

As an alternative, you can use custom PHP code to perform the changes directly within an import and modify the data on-the-fly.

For example, if you need to add or append extra content to the existing content, you can create a function to do so. You can call this function in Step 5 (from the guide above) when setting up the import template.

For examples of this, see [Calling PHP Functions in Your Import Configuration](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/inline-php/).

page_title: How to Bulk Edit WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: Solve the "There's a problem with your import file" Error
text:
### Solve the "There's a problem with your import file" Error

In some cases, you may see the following message:

![Bulk Edit WordPress Data Error with Import File](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/Bulk-Edit-WordPress-Data-Error-with-Import-File-1024x991.png)

This message means that the structure of the import file (i.e., the columns) has changed in some way.

To solve this error, review your import file and make sure that all column names and the file structure itself remain the same as in the exported file (see Step 4 in the guide above).

If you're not entirely sure what's wrong, you can download a fresh copy of the export, repeat the bulk edits, then re-import everything again.

page_title: How to Bulk Edit WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: Bulk Edit WooCommerce and WordPress – Frequently Asked Questions
text: ## Bulk Edit WooCommerce and WordPress – Frequently Asked Questions

page_title: How to Bulk Edit WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: How Do I Bulk Edit in WooCommerce?
text:
### How Do I Bulk Edit in WooCommerce?

To bulk edit in WooCommerce, follow the same basic steps outlined in this tutorial, except select one of the WooCommerce post types for the initial export. The rest of the process is more or less the same.

page_title: How to Bulk Edit WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: Can I Bulk Edit WooCommerce Products?
text:
### Can I Bulk Edit WooCommerce Products?

Bulk editing WooCommerce products is no different than bulk editing any other type of WordPress or WooCommerce data. All you have to do is choose **WooCommerce Products** at the start of the process described in this guide. Otherwise, the process is the same.

For more information, see [How to Bulk Edit WooCommerce Products](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-bulk-edit-woocommerce-products/).

page_title: How to Bulk Edit WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: How Do I Bulk Edit Product Variations?
text:
### How Do I Bulk Edit Product Variations?

1. Create a WooCommere product export.
2. Product variations are included in the export by default.
3. Complete the export.
4. Edit the variations data in your favorite spreadsheet.
5. Import the changes back into WooCommerce.

For more information, see the [Bulk Edit Product Variations Cheat Sheet](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-do-i-bulk-edit-product-variations-in-woocommerce/).

page_title: How to Bulk Edit WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: How Do I Bulk Edit Pages in WordPress?
text:
### How Do I Bulk Edit Pages in WordPress?

1. Go to **All Export › New Export** and select **Pages** as your export type.
2. Choose the export columns.
3. Run the export and download the export file.
4. Bulk edit the pages in your favorite spreadsheet.
5. Import the changes using the **Import with WP All Import** option.
6. Confirm that the changes were properly applied.

page_title: How to Bulk Edit WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: How Do I Bulk Edit Image Metadata?
text:
### How Do I Bulk Edit Image Metadata?

1. Export the post type that includes the image metadata that you want to edit.
2. Include the **Available Data › Media › Images** metadata fields in your export.
3. Bulk edit the metadata fields in a spreadsheet.
4. Image the changed metadata fields back into WooCommerce or WordPress.
5. Verify that your changes were applied.

To learn more, see [Bulk Editing Image Metadata Cheat Sheet](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-do-i-bulk-edit-image-metadata/).

page_title: How to Bulk Edit WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: How Do I Bulk Edit WordPress or WooCommerce Categories?
text:
### How Do I Bulk Edit WordPress or WooCommerce Categories?

There are two methods of doing this. To bulk edit WordPress or WooCommerce categories on their own, you can follow a process similar to the one described in this guide. Just select the appropriate category at the start of the export process. See [How to Export WordPress Categories and Tags](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-export-wordpress-categories-tags/) for more information.

Alternatively, you can [include category information when exporting another post type](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/export-posts-with-their-taxonomies/) (such as WordPress posts or WooCommerce products). If you modify this category information, you can tell WP All Import how to apply the changes during the import process.

page_title: How to Bulk Edit WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: How Do I Migrate WordPress or WooCommerce Data?
text:
### How Do I Migrate WordPress or WooCommerce Data?

1. On the source site, export the data in question but download a bundle file instead of just the data file.
2. On the destination site, upload the bundle file. The bundle file contains all the instructions needed to configure the import.
3. Run the import.
4. Verify that the data has been migrated as expected.

To learn more, see [How to Migrate WooCommerce and WordPress Data](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-migrate-woocommerce-and-wordpress-data/).

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Variable Products
heading_path: Step 1: Create a New Product Export
text:
## Step 1: Create a New Product Export

Go to **All Export › New Export** and select **WooCommerce Products** from the available list of post types.

![Export Variable Products New Export](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Export-Variable-Products-New-Export-1024x838.jpg)

Export filters can be added using the option **Add Filtering Options**. To learn more, see [Filter Exported WordPress Data](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/filter-exported-wordpress-data/).

Since we're exporting only variable products, we will add a filter to only export those products:

**Element**: Product Type
**Rule**: In Product Type
**Value**: variable

![Export Variable Products Filter](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Export-Variable-Products-Filter-1024x543.jpg)

Click **Customize Export File** to continue.

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Variable Products
heading_path: Step 2: Customize Your Export File and Include Attributes Fields
text:
## Step 2: Customize Your Export File and Include Attributes Fields

You now see the **Drag & Drop** interface where you can build your export file by dragging export fields from the **Available Data** section into the column selection area.

![Export Variable Products Customize Export Fields Example](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/Export-Variable-Products-Customize-Export-Fields-Example-1024x841.jpg)

You can also remove any field by dragging it and dropping it from the column selection area to any other interface part.

To add all available fields, simply click on **Add All**. You can also preview the selected data with the **Preview** green button.

![Export Variable Products Preview](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Export-Variable-Products-Preview-1024x558.png)

This guide focuses on exporting WooCommerce variable products. As such, we will explain the export fields associated with variable products specifically.

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Variable Products
heading_path: Step 2: Customize Your Export File and Include Attributes Fields
text:
ooCommerce variable products. As such, we will explain the export fields associated with variable products specifically.

If you want to learn more about the other export fields available for WooCommerce products, please see [Understanding WooCommerce Product Export Fields](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-export-woocommerce-products-to-csv-or-xml/#products-export-fields).

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Variable Products
heading_path: WooCommerce Product Data
text:
### WooCommerce Product Data

The principal WooCommerce product fields are found in **Available Data › Product Data**:

- Sku: A product code used to reference and differentiate products.
- Price: The product's current price, which is the regular price, unless the sale price is active and lower.
- Regular Price: The regular, full price of the product.
- Sale Price: The sale price of the product.
- Stock Status: One of several preset values. The default WooCommerce stock statuses are 
```
instock
```

, 
```
outofstock
```

, and 
```
onbackorder
```

.
- Stock: The number of products currently in stock and available for sale.
- External Product URL: The affiliate link for external products.
- Total Sales: Number of times the product has been sold.
- Attributes: This will export the attribute's name, value, whether it's used in variations, is visible, and is a taxonomy. This export element creates five columns per attribute. This data is more easily exported in the **Attributes** section.
- Shipping Class: If configured, it exports the name of the product's shipping class.

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Variable Products
heading_path: WooCommerce Product Data
text:
ted in the **Attributes** section.
- Shipping Class: If configured, it exports the name of the product's shipping class.

Variable products have different attributes, such as size or color. Those attributes will be automatically detected and added to the **Attributes** section at the bottom of **Product Data**.

![Export Variable Products Attributes Section](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Export-Variable-Products-Attributes-Section-1024x671.jpg)

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Variable Products
heading_path: Advanced Options
text:
### Advanced Options

You will see an **Advanced Options** section with the following settings:

- **Separator**: Used for CSV files. Defaults to a comma (,).
- **CSV Header Row**: Has the option **Include header row and column titles in export** enabled by default. When disabled, the CSV file would have no header row.
- **Product Variations**: these settings decide how to export the product variations, with the following options available (which are self-explanatory):
- Export product variations and their parent products
- Product variations use the parent product title
- Product variations use the default variation product title
- Only export product variations
- Product variations use the parent product title
- Product variations use the default variation product title
- Only export parent products

![Export Variable Products Advanced Options](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Export-Variable-Products-Advanced-Options-1024x940.jpg)

Once the export is configured, click the blue **Continue** button at the bottom. You'll now see the **Export Settings**screen.

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Variable Products
heading_path: Step 3: Configure the Export Settings
text:
## Step 3: Configure the Export Settings

![Export Variable Products Settings](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Export-Variable-Products-Settings-1024x717.jpg)

Near the top of this screen, WP All Export shows a summary of what will be exported. You can also define **Scheduling Options** here and change other **Advanced Options** for the export. For example, you can set the batch processing size, control whether the export should be split into multiple output files, and even dictate the behavior for subsequent runs of the same export.

In this case, we'll leave all of these export settings as they are and click **Confirm & Run Export** to start the export process.

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Variable Products
heading_path: Step 4: Download Your Export File
text:
## Step 4: Download Your Export File

Once the export finishes, you'll see an export complete message along with different options to download the exported file. You can download the selected file type (i.e., CSV, Excel, XML, Google Sheets) or a **Bundle** compressed zip file that has the export file along with a template to use with WP All Import.

![Export Variable Products Export Complete](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Export-Variable-Products-Export-Complete-1024x604.jpg)

That's how you export WooCommerce variable products.

![](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/plugins/custom-acf-blocks/blocks/docs-interstitial/img/octopus-logo.svg)

heading_path: Automatically Export WooCommerce Variable Products on a Schedule
text:
### Automatically Export WooCommerce Variable Products on a Schedule

With WP All Export, you can schedule WooCommerce product export using one of these scheduling options:

- Manual Scheduling: set up manual cron jobs via your control panel (such as cPanel or Plesk) that will run your export on a schedule. Learn more in [Schedule WordPress Exports Using Cron Jobs](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/scheduling-exports-using-cron-jobs/).
- Automatic Scheduling: this is our proprietary Automatic Scheduling Service, which has a small monthly fee. Its big advantage is that you can control scheduling via WP All Export's interface: [Run WordPress Export on a Schedule](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/run-wordpress-export-on-a-schedule/).

heading_path: Export WooCommerce Variable Products — Frequently Asked Questions
text: ## Export WooCommerce Variable Products — Frequently Asked Questions

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Variable Products
heading_path: What is a WooCommerce Product Attribute?
text:
### What is a WooCommerce Product Attribute?

A WooCommerce attribute describes a characteristic or feature of a product, such as size or color. Attributes have terms, such as "blue", "green", etc., for a color attribute. Attribute terms can be combined to create variations. For example, the attribute term "blue" can be combined with the term "XL" to make a blue XL t-shirt variation.

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Variable Products
heading_path: What is a WooCommerce Variable Product?
text:
### What is a WooCommerce Variable Product?

A WooCommerce variable product is a product that is available in multiple variations, such as a t-shirt that comes in different colors and sizes. These variations are created using attributes. Each variation can have its own stock, price, images, etc.

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Variable Products
heading_path: How Do I Get Product Variation Data in WooCommerce?
text:
### How Do I Get Product Variation Data in WooCommerce?

To "get product variation data in WooCommerce" is just another way of saying that you want to export WooCommerce variable products, so all you have to do is follow the steps in this tutorial.

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Variable Products
heading_path: How Do I Export All Products and Variations from WooCommerce?
text:
### How Do I Export All Products and Variations from WooCommerce?

To export all products and variations from WooCommerce, follow this guide but make sure that you don't set any product filters. This will ensure that your export includes all WooCommerce products and variations.

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Variable Products
heading_path: How Do I Bulk Edit Variable Products in WooCommerce?
text:
### How Do I Bulk Edit Variable Products in WooCommerce?

1. Export variable product data in either the Excel or CSV format.
2. Use your favorite spreadsheet to make bulk changes to exported data.
3. Import the changed data back into WooCommerce using WP All Import.
4. Verify that the changes were correctly applied.

For more information, see [How Do I Bulk Edit Product Variations in WooCommerce?](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-do-i-bulk-edit-product-variations-in-woocommerce/)

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Variable Products
heading_path: How Do I Import Product Variations in WooCommerce?
text:
### How Do I Import Product Variations in WooCommerce?

1. Go to **All Import › New Import**.
2. Select how to import the data (i.e., upload a file, download a file from a URL, etc.).
3. Choose **WooCommmerce Products** as the import target.
4. Map incoming data elements to your WooCommerce target fields with drag & drop.
5. Use the **I'm importing new variations to existing products** option in the **Variations** tab.
6. Perform the import.

To see the list of full import features, see [Import WooCommerce Products](https://www.wpallimport.com/woocommerce-product-import).

heading_path: How Do I Export WooCommerce Products to Excel?
text:
### How Do I Export WooCommerce Products to Excel?

1. Go to **All Export > New Export**.
2. Choose **WooCommerce Products** from the dropdown list.
3. Set up the export file by customizing the export columns.
4. Change the file format in **Export Type > Spreadsheet > Excel File**.
5. Run the export and download the Excel file.

To learn more, see [Export WooCommerce Products](https://www.wpallimport.com/woocommerce-product-export/).

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Variable Products
heading_path: How Do I Export My WordPress Product Feed for Google Shopping?
text:
### How Do I Export My WordPress Product Feed for Google Shopping?

1. Navigate to **All Export › New Export** and choose **WooCommerce Products**.
2. Modify the **Export Type** to **Feed › Google Merchant Center Product Feed**.
3. Set up the Google Merchant Center export as required.
4. Upload the exported .TXT file to Google.

For more information, see [Export WooCommerce Products to Google Shopping](https://www.wpallimport.com/woocommerce-google-shopping/).

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Variable Products
heading_path: How to Export WooCommerce Attributes in a Multilingual Environment Using Cron Jobs?
text:
### How to Export WooCommerce Attributes in a Multilingual Environment Using Cron Jobs?

If you're exporting WooCommerce attributes in a multilingual environment and want to use cron jobs or Automatic Scheduling to automate the export, you should configure the export template while logged into a WordPress user account that uses the site's default language. This is necessary because WooCommerce adds the word "Product" to the attribute elements based on the language of the current user. Since automatic exports use the site's default language, configuring the export with a user that does not use the site's default language will result in the attribute elements being blank.

page_title: How to Export WooCommerce Variable Products
heading_path: What Add-Ons Do I Need to Export WooCommerce Products?
text:
### What Add-Ons Do I Need to Export WooCommerce Products?

To export WooCommerce products, you need to have the WooCommerce Export Add-On active on your site, along with WP All Export.

page_title: How to Migrate WordPress Data
heading_path: How to Migrate WordPress Data
text:
**WP All Export** and **WP All Import** can work together to let you migrate WordPress data with ease. You can migrate any post type (such as WordPress posts, pages, etc.) or custom post type (such as WooCommerce products or property listings) that exists on your site.

This process allows you to migrate all the data from your selected post type into a different target site. Moreover, you can perform any change or modification to the migrated data, either by modifying the file manually or using PHP functions.

Here's a video that shows how a full migration process works:

Below, you can learn more about each of our documentation available for migrations:

Quickstart guide that explains to you how to migrate WooCommerce and WordPress data.

Step-by-step instructions on how to migrate WordPress users from one site to another.

Elaborates on how to migrate WooCommerce products to a different WordPress website.

Shows how to migrate WooCommerce customers to a different site.

Explains the process of migrating WooCommerce orders.

Learn how to migrate Gravity Forms Entries successfully.

page_title: How to Migrate WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: How to Migrate WooCommerce and WordPress Data
text: To migrate WooCommerce and WordPress data from one site to another, export it from the source site using WP All Export, then import it on the destination site with WP All Import. Because these two plugins understand each other, this can be done with just a few clicks.

page_title: How to Migrate WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: Step 1: Select the Migration Post Type
text:
## Step 1: Select the Migration Post Type

Navigate to **All Export › New Export** and choose the type of data that you want to migrate.

![Migrate WooCommerce WordPress Data New Export](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Migrate-WooCommerce-WordPress-Data-New-Export-1014x1024.jpg)

You can migrate WooCommerce and WordPress data of all types, even from custom themes and plugins.

Once you choose what to export, click **Customize Export File** at the bottom of the screen (not shown).

page_title: How to Migrate WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: Step 2: Choose the Data Columns to Export
text:
## Step 2: Choose the Data Columns to Export

The next screen to appear is the **Drag & Drop** interface. In this screen, the fields available to export are listed on the right. Drag and drop those you want to migrate into the column selection area, or click **Add All** to include everything.

![Migrate WooCommerce WordPress Data Choose What to Export](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Migrate-WooCommerce-WordPress-Data-Choose-What-to-Export-1024x775.jpg)

As with a regular export, you can rename and rearrange the fields as needed. You can also use PHP code to modify the export data.

Once you've finished selecting and configuring your export columns, click **Continue** (not shown).

page_title: How to Migrate WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: Step 3: Confirm & Run the Export
text:
## Step 3: Confirm & Run the Export

Verify the final export settings, then click on **Confirm & Run Export**.

![Migrate WooCommerce WordPress Data Confirm Run Export](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/Migrate-WooCommerce-WordPress-Data-Confirm-Run-Export-1024x901.png)

page_title: How to Migrate WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: Step 4: Download the Export Bundle for Migrations
text:
## Step 4: Download the Export Bundle for Migrations

Download the export bundle ZIP file using the **Bundle** button. This file is designed to support WooCommerce and WordPress migrations, as it contains an import template in addition to the export data. There's no need to unzip the file.

![Migrate WooCommerce WordPress Data Download Export Bundle](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Migrate-WooCommerce-WordPress-Data-Download-Export-Bundle-1024x606.jpg)

page_title: How to Migrate WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: Step 5: Upload the Export Bundle to WP All Import
text:
## Step 5: Upload the Export Bundle to WP All Import

Start a new import at **All Import › New Import** on your destination site, then upload the ZIP bundle file that you downloaded in the previous step.

![Migrate WooCommerce WordPress Data New Import](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Migrate-WooCommerce-WordPress-Data-New-Import--1024x370.jpg)

By using the export bundle, it automatically configures the import, allowing you to click **Skip to Import Settings**.

![Migrate WooCommerce WordPress Data Skip to Import Settings](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/Migrate-WooCommerce-WordPress-Data-Skip-to-Import-Settings-1024x667.png)

page_title: How to Migrate WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: Step 6: Confirm & Run the Import to Complete the Migration
text:
## Step 6: Confirm & Run the Import to Complete the Migration

The import settings shouldn't need any changes — just click **Continue**. Then click **Confirm & Run Import** on the following page to run the import.

![Migrate WooCommerce WordPress Data Confirm Run Import](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Migrate-WooCommerce-WordPress-Data-Confirm-Run-Import-1024x599.jpg)

That's all you have to do to migrate WooCommerce and WordPress data. It really is that simple!

![](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/plugins/custom-acf-blocks/blocks/docs-interstitial/img/octopus-logo.svg)

page_title: How to Migrate WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: Using Filters to Migrate Only Some Records
text:
### Using Filters to Migrate Only Some Records

You can use filters in two different places:

- WP All Export, to only export certain selected records. For example, to limit the exported WordPress posts to those which contain "Offer" in the title. See [Filter Exported WordPress Data](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/filter-exported-wordpress-data/).
- WP All Import, to select which records to import. For example, to limit the imported WooCommerce products to those which have a price equal to or higher than 30. See [Filtering with XPath](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/filtering-with-xpath/).

By using either export or import filters, you can decide which records will be migrated. Either option will work.

page_title: How to Migrate WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: Migrate WooCommerce or WordPress Data from Third-Party Plugins or Themes
text:
### Migrate WooCommerce or WordPress Data from Third-Party Plugins or Themes

When you include all fields in Step 2 (see above), this will also migrate all custom fields created by third-party plugins or themes. However, this will only include custom fields that are stored in the regular WordPress post meta database table.

If the data is stored in a custom database table, you'll have to use custom code ([see documentation](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/inline-php/)) along with our API ([see documentation](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/action-reference/)) to migrate the data. Here's an example for the import portion of this: [Import Data to Custom Database Table](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/code-snippets/#import-data-to-custom-database-table-during-record-import).

page_title: How to Migrate WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: Difference Between Migrating Posts or Other Post Types
text:
### Difference Between Migrating Posts or Other Post Types

This Quickstart guide shows how to migrate WordPress posts, but the same steps can be used to migrate any post type or custom post type.

The main impact of migrating different post types is that the plugin options will change according to the post type being imported or exported.

page_title: How to Migrate WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: Migrate WooCommerce and WordPress Data – Frequently Asked Questions
text: ## Migrate WooCommerce and WordPress Data – Frequently Asked Questions

page_title: How to Migrate WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: What Does It Mean to Migrate a WordPress Site?
text:
### What Does It Mean to Migrate a WordPress Site?

Migrating a WordPress site means moving all or part of it from one host or domain to another while retaining all functionality. The most common reasons for this are to change hosts or to upgrade to a new version of a website.

page_title: How to Migrate WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: Does WordPress Offer Free Migration?
text:
### Does WordPress Offer Free Migration?

WordPress does not offer free migration as a service. However, you can perform a basic migration yourself for free by following the [Moving WordPress](https://wordpress.org/support/article/moving-wordpress/) guide. The drawback of this option is that it uses an "all or nothing" approach that gives you very little control over the migration details.

page_title: How to Migrate WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: How Do I Manually Migrate a WordPress Site to a New Host?
text:
### How Do I Manually Migrate a WordPress Site to a New Host?

To manually migrate the whole site, including files and its database, follow the [Moving WordPress](https://wordpress.org/support/article/moving-wordpress/) guide. If you want to move part of your website with complete control over the migration of each part, then just follow the steps in this tutorial.

page_title: How to Migrate WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: How Do I Maintain Links Between the Migrated Data?
text:
### How Do I Maintain Links Between the Migrated Data?

Our plugins can maintain all relationships and links that exist between your migrated data. For example, when you migrate WooCommerce customers, products, and orders, the relationship between them is maintained as long as you migrate the orders last.

page_title: How to Migrate WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: How Do I Keep the Same IDs When Migrating WooCommerce or WordPress Data?
text:
### How Do I Keep the Same IDs When Migrating WooCommerce or WordPress Data?

You can achieve this using custom code ([see documentation](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/inline-php/)) along with our API ([see documentation](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/action-reference/)). When you migrate WooCommerce data (such as products or orders) or WordPress data (such as posts or pages), you can apply the following workaround: [Keep old IDs when migrating products or posts](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/code-snippets/#keep-old-ids-when-migrating-products).

This workaround attempts to maintain the old ID, but it's only an attempt. If the requested ID is not available, a new ID is generated upon import.

page_title: How to Migrate WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: Will Your Plugins Migrate Taxonomies, Images, and Custom Fields, Too?
text:
### Will Your Plugins Migrate Taxonomies, Images, and Custom Fields, Too?

Yes. Our plugins will automatically migrate the taxonomies attached to the post type being migrated. This includes default taxonomies (such as categories or tags) along with custom taxonomies (added by a third-party plugin or with custom code).

The same is true for all images and all custom fields that are attached to the migrated records.

page_title: How to Migrate WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: How Do I Migrate WooCommerce Customers and Orders?
text:
### How Do I Migrate WooCommerce Customers and Orders?

In general, follow the instructions in this guide but select **WooCommerce Customers** and **WooCommerce Orders** as the post types (i.e., two separate migrations with customers first and orders last). For specific instructions, see [How to Migrate WooCommerce Customers to a Different Site](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-migrate-woocommerce-customers/) and [How to Migrate WooCommerce Orders](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-migrate-woocommerce-orders/).

page_title: How to Migrate WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: How Do I Migrate WooCommerce Products?
text:
### How Do I Migrate WooCommerce Products?

1. Export your WooCommerce products using WP All Export.
2. Download your export file using the **Bundle** option. The bundle file contains an import template in addition to the product data.
3. Upload the bundle file on the target site.
4. The bundle file will instruct WP All Import on how to import the products.
5. Run the import and check your imported products.

For additional information, see [Migrate WooCommerce Products](https://www.wpallimport.com/migrate-woocommerce-products/).

page_title: How to Migrate WooCommerce and WordPress Data
heading_path: How Do I Migrate WordPress Users?
text:
### How Do I Migrate WordPress Users?

1. Export the users that you wish to migrate.
2. Download the **Bundle** option, which contains an import template in addition to the export data.
3. Import the bundle file. WP All Import will automatically configure itself based on this file.
4. Run the import.
5. Check the imported users.

To learn more, see [How to Migrate WordPress Users from One WordPress Site to Another](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-migrate-wordpress-users/).

page_title: WordPress Export Posts with Images
heading_path: Step 1: Create a New Export for WordPress Posts
text:
## Step 1: Create a New Export for WordPress Posts

Navigate to **All Export › New Export** and select **Posts** from the dropdown list.

![Export WordPress Posts with Images New Export](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/Export-Posts-with-Images-New-Export-1024x677.jpg)

When needed, **Add Filtering Options** to control which WordPress posts will be exported.

Click on **Customize Export File** to continue.

page_title: WordPress Export Posts with Images
heading_path: Step 2: Customize the Export Layout to Include Image Data
text:
## Step 2: Customize the Export Layout to Include Image Data

The **Drag & Drop** interface appears, where you build the export template.

To include data in an export, drag fields from the **Available Data** section on the right and drop them into the column selection area on the left:

![Export WordPress Posts with Images Drag and Drop Interface](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/Export-Posts-with-Images-Drag-and-Drop-Interface-1024x562.jpg)

To remove an export column, drag it out of the column selection area and drop it anywhere else on the interface.

To learn about the different sections available when exporting posts, you can review [Customize the WordPress Post Export File](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/export-wordpress-posts/#customize-posts-export).

In this document, we will focus on the **Media** section, which contains the image and attachment fields associated with WordPress posts. To include images in your export, you must drag fields from this section into the column selection area.

The remainder of this section provides detailed descriptions of the relevant fields.

page_title: WordPress Export Posts with Images
heading_path: Media › Images
text:
### Media › Images

The data associated with images on your WordPress posts will appear here. The following fields are available:

- Image URL: URL to the image.
- Image Filename: Filename of the full-size image.
- Image Path: Path or location (on the server) to the image.
- Image ID: The attachment ID of the image.
- Image Title: The image title.
- Image Caption: The image caption.
- Image Description: The image description.
- Image Alt Text: The image alternative text.
- Image Featured: The URL of the featured image.

Multiple values are separated by a pipe "|" character.

![Export WordPress Posts with Images Media Image Data](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/Export-Posts-with-Images-Media-Image-Data-1024x764.jpg)

page_title: WordPress Export Posts with Images
heading_path: Media › Attachments
text:
### Media › Attachments

The attachments uploaded to your WordPress site generate the same types of fields, as shown below:

- Attachment URL: URL to the attachment.
- Attachment Filename: Filename of the attachment.
- Attachment Path: Path or location (on the server) to the attachment.
- Attachment ID: The attachment ID of the attachment.
- Attachment Title: Attachment's title.
- Attachment Caption: Attachment's caption.
- Attachment Description: Attachment's description.
- Attachment Alt Text: Attachment's alternative text.

If there are multiple attachments per post, the different values are again separated by a pipe "|" character.

![Export WordPress Posts with Images Media Attachments Data](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/Export-Posts-with-Images-Media-Attachments-Data-1024x765.jpg)

page_title: WordPress Export Posts with Images
heading_path: Media from Custom Fields
text:
### Media from Custom Fields

If your media is stored in a different way, i.e., embedded in a serialized array inside a custom field, you can still export it. You just have to write custom PHP code as explained in the guide: [How to Pass Exported WordPress Data Through PHP Functions](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/custom-wordpress-export-php/).

Basically, you must apply custom code to that custom field. Your code should process the data entirely, locate the specific data that you wish to extract, and then return it.

While we may be able to provide examples in some scenarios, we don't help with custom coding. For that type of assistance, you can reach out to one of our [recommended developers](https://www.wpallimport.com/developers/).

page_title: WordPress Export Posts with Images
heading_path: Step 3: Configure the Export Settings
text:
## Step 3: Configure the Export Settings

After setting up the export template, click on the **Continue** button at the bottom of the page (not shown). Next, you'll see the **Export Settings** screen:

![Export WordPress Posts with Images Export Settings](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Export-Posts-with-Images-Export-Settings-1024x794.jpg)

This interface lets you modify the default export settings. You can add **Scheduling Options** or change **Advanced Options**. Once you're ready to run the WordPress posts export, click on **Confirm & Run Export**.

page_title: WordPress Export Posts with Images
heading_path: Step 4: Download the Exported Posts with Images
text:
## Step 4: Download the Exported Posts with Images

You will see the following screen once the export completes:

![Export WordPress Posts with Images Export Complete](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Export-Posts-with-Images-Export-Complete-1024x774.jpg)

On this screen, you can download the exported file in the selected format (i.e., **CSV**, **XML**, **Excel**) or as a **Bundle**file that you can use for migrations. You can also use **External Apps** to send the data to Google Sheets.

You can schedule a WordPress posts export using either of our integrated **Scheduling Options**:

text:

To learn more, visit: [How to Send Exported WordPress Data to Zapier](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/external-app-integration/).

heading_path: Export WordPress Posts with Images — Frequently Asked Questions
text: ## Export WordPress Posts with Images — Frequently Asked Questions

page_title: WordPress Export Posts with Images
heading_path: How Do I Export Posts from WordPress?
text:
### How Do I Export Posts from WordPress?

1. Create a new export and choose **Posts**.
2. Select the export fields that you want to include in the export.
3. Run the export.
4. Download the exported WordPress Posts.
5. Review the contents of the export file.

To learn more, see [How to Export WordPress Posts](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/export-wordpress-posts/).

page_title: WordPress Export Posts with Images
heading_path: Does a WordPress Post Export Include Images?
text:
### Does a WordPress Post Export Include Images?

You can use the procedures described in this document to include images with your post export or any other post type that has images or attachments. You can even export media stored in custom fields.

For a broader description of our export capabilities, see [Export WordPress to CSV, XML, Excel or Google Sheets](https://www.wpallimport.com/export-wordpress/).

page_title: WordPress Export Posts with Images
heading_path: How Do I Export All Images from WordPress?
text:
### How Do I Export All Images from WordPress?

You cannot export the entire Media Library out of the box. You have to export the post type or custom post type and then include its associated images in the export, exactly as we've demonstrated in this document.

However, there is a workaround using a WP_Query export ([see documentation](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/wp-query-export-to-csv/)) to generate an export file with all the media or attachments that exist on your site. This is the query:

```
'post_type' => 'attachment', 
'post_status' => 'inherit'
```

page_title: WordPress Export Posts with Images
heading_path: How Do I Export All My WordPress Posts with Images?
text:
### How Do I Export All My WordPress Posts with Images?

To export all your posts with images, just export your posts exactly as described in this guide, including image fields, but without applying any filters. This is the beauty of WP All Export. It handles image fields the same way it handles most fields.

page_title: WordPress Export Posts with Images
heading_path: How Do I Export a Featured Image with a WordPress Post?
text:
### How Do I Export a Featured Image with a WordPress Post?

To export a featured image with a post, just include the **Image Featured** field from the **Available Data > Media > Images** section in your export. There are no other required actions.

page_title: WordPress Export Posts with Images
heading_path: How Do I Import WordPress Posts with Images?
text:
### How Do I Import WordPress Posts with Images?

To import WordPress posts with images, you need to map the incoming image data elements to their corresponding image fields. However, the way that you do this can vary significantly depending on where the images are stored.

For more information, see [How to Import WordPress Posts with Images](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-import-wordpress-posts-with-images/).

page_title: How to Export WordPress Posts
heading_path: How to Export WordPress Posts
text: To export WordPress posts, go to **WP All Export › New Export** and select **Posts** from the dropdown list. Build your export file using the **Drag & Drop** interface. Once done, export the posts and download the exported file.

page_title: How to Export WordPress Posts
heading_path: Table of Contents
text:
## Table of Contents

- [Step 3: Configure the Export Settings](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/export-wordpress-posts/#configure-export-settings)

**Advanced Topics**

page_title: How to Export WordPress Posts
heading_path: Step 1: Create a New Export for WordPress Posts
text:
## Step 1: Create a New Export for WordPress Posts

Go to **All Export › New Export** on the site, choose **Posts**, and WP All Export will update to show the number of posts it will export:

![Export WordPress Posts New Export](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/Export-WordPress-Posts-New-Export-1024x762.jpg)

Below this section, you can **Add Filtering Options** to make the export more selective.

Once you've defined which posts to export, click on **Customize Export File**.

page_title: How to Export WordPress Posts
heading_path: Step 2: Choose Your Export Columns
text:
## Step 2: Choose Your Export Columns

The **Drag & Drop** interface will appear, which lets you decide which data columns to export and allows you to modify the export file format.

The data available for export is shown in the **Available Data** section on the right. To add a field to the export, drag it from that section to the column selection area on the left:

![Export WordPress Posts Drag and Drop](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/Export-WordPress-Posts-Drag-and-Drop-1024x791.jpg)

You can delete an export column by dragging it outside the column selection area, or by clicking on the column and then clicking on **Delete**.

When exporting WordPress posts, you will see the following sections: **Standard**, **Media**, **Taxonomies**, **Custom Fields**, and **Other**.

page_title: How to Export WordPress Posts
heading_path: Posts Standard Data
text:
### Posts Standard Data

WordPress post standard data is found in **Available Data › Standard**. The following data fields are available:

- ID: The post ID is a unique number given to every post in WordPress.
- Title: The title of the WordPress post.
- Content: The post's content.
- Excerpt: A short description of the post.
- Date: The date the product was published, in y-m-d H:i:s format.
- Post Type: The post type to export, which is 
```
post
```

 in this case.
- Permaink: The URL of the post.

![Export WordPress Posts Standard Post Data](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/Export-WordPress-Posts-Standard-Post-Data-1024x701.jpg)

page_title: How to Export WordPress Posts
heading_path: Posts Media
text:
### Posts Media

All Images and attachments associated with the exported WordPress posts will appear here. You will see two sections inside the **Media** section: **Images** and **Attachments**. Both sections contain the same types of fields:

- URL: The URL of the image or file.
- Filename: The filename for the full-size image or the file.
- Path: The path or location on the server of the image or file.
- ID: The attachment ID of the image or file, similar to a post ID.
- Title: The image or file title.
- Caption: The image or file caption.
- Description: The image or file description.
- Alt Text: The image or file's alternative text.
- Featured: The URL of the featured image (not shown for attachments).

![Export WordPress Posts Media](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/Export-WordPress-Posts-Media-1024x792.jpg)

page_title: How to Export WordPress Posts
heading_path: Posts Taxonomies
text:
### Posts Taxonomies

This section includes the categories and tags attached to the WordPress posts. By default, posts only have two taxonomies, but all taxonomies are detected and shown here during export:

- Categories
- Tags

![Export WordPress Posts Taxonomies](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/Export-WordPress-Posts-Taxonomies-1024x669.jpg)

page_title: How to Export WordPress Posts
heading_path: Posts Custom Fields
text:
### Posts Custom Fields

This section contains any custom fields added by custom code or other plugins. This interface will show up to 1000 custom fields, but custom code can be used to retrieve any custom field.

![Export WordPress Posts Custom Fields](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/Export-WordPress-Posts-Custom-Fields-1024x795.jpg)

page_title: How to Export WordPress Posts
heading_path: Other Posts Data
text:
### Other Posts Data

This section contains other post data not included in the other sections. The following is an incomplete list with only the more important other export fields:

- Status: The post status. The default post statuses are 
```
publish
```

, 
```
draft
```

, 
```
pending
```

, and 
```
private
```

.
- Author ID: The ID of the user that created the post.
- Author Username: The username of the user that created the post.
- Slug: The post's slug.
- Parent: If the post has a parent post, it will export its ID. Otherwise, 
```
0
```

 is exported.
- Parent Slug: If the post has a parent, it will export its slug. Otherwise, 
```
0
```

 is exported.
- Post Modified Date: The date the product was last modified, in y-m-d H:i:s format.

![Export WordPress Posts Other Data](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/Export-WordPress-Posts-Other-Data-1024x789.jpg)

page_title: How to Export WordPress Posts
heading_path: Step 3: Configure the Export Settings
text:
## Step 3: Configure the Export Settings

Once the export template is set up, click **Continue** at the bottom. This will bring you to the **Export Settings** screen, where you will see the following:

![Export Settings](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/Export-WordPress-Posts-Export-Settings-1024x788.jpg)

Here you can configure **Scheduling Options** and **Advanced Options**. When you are ready to run your export, click **Confirm & Run Export**.

page_title: How to Export WordPress Posts
heading_path: Step 4: Download the Exported WordPress Posts
text:
## Step 4: Download the Exported WordPress Posts

The export will begin to process. Once it is complete, you can download the exported file in either the selected format (i.e., **CSV**, **XML**, **Excel**) or as a **Bundle**file. You can also send the export data using **External Apps** to save it in Google Sheets. In addition to the export data, the bundle file contains instructions that tell WP All Import how to import the data.

![Confirm and Run Screen](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/Export-WordPress-Posts-Confirm-and-Run-1024x775.jpg)

Here's a look at an example WordPress posts export:

![Export WordPress Posts Example File](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/Export-WordPress-Posts-Example-Posts-Export-1024x373.jpg)

![](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/plugins/custom-acf-blocks/blocks/docs-interstitial/img/octopus-logo.svg)

heading_path: Export WordPress Posts —Advanced Topics
text: ## Export WordPress Posts —Advanced Topics

page_title: How to Export WordPress Posts
heading_path: Add Filters to Your WordPress Post Export
text:
### Add Filters to Your WordPress Post Export

You can add a filter to export only a subset of WordPress posts. The filter can be used on all export fields described in Step 2 of the guide above.

To filter your WordPress posts, expand **Add Filtering Options** in the panel at the bottom of Step 1, then choose an **Element**, select a **Rule**, and input a **Value**:

![Export WordPress Posts Add Filtering Options](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/Export-WordPress-Posts-Add-Filtering-Options-1024x377.jpg)

In this example, you are setting:

Element = Date

Rule = equal to or newer than

Value = yesterday

This is the same as saying, “Export posts with a date equal to or earlier than 'yesterday.'"

You can also combine multiple filters. For example, to export posts with a category of "News" that have a status of publish, set your filters like this:

![Export WordPress Posts Multiple Filters](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/Export-WordPress-Posts-Multiple-Filters-1024x350.jpg)

page_title: How to Export WordPress Posts
heading_path: Add Filters to Your WordPress Post Export
text:
le Filters](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/Export-WordPress-Posts-Multiple-Filters-1024x350.jpg)

The filters can be as complex as you want. Using the same **Filtering Options** section, you can modify existing filters in **All Export** › **Manage Exports** › **Settings**.

page_title: How to Export WordPress Posts
heading_path: Choose A Different Export File Format
text:
### Choose A Different Export File Format

You can choose a different export type to output a different file format. The following options are available:

- Spreadsheet
- CSV File
- Excel File (XLS)
- Excel File (XLSX)
- Feed
- Simple XML Feed
- Custom XML Feed

You can learn more about using any of the available export types here: [Export WordPress to CSV, Excel, XML, or Google Sheets](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/export-wordpress-to-csv-excel-or-xml/).

You can export this data to Google Sheets using Zapier and our third-party integration.

page_title: How to Export WordPress Posts
heading_path: Learn How To Connect Your Export With External Apps
text:
### Learn How To Connect Your Export With External Apps

You can use Zapier to connect with external apps. Zapier acts as a middle man between WP All Export and hundreds of other popular apps. For example, you can send the exported posts via email (i.e., using [Gmail](https://zapier.com/apps/wp-all-export-pro/integrations/gmail)) or to [Google Drive](https://zapier.com/apps/wp-all-export-pro/integrations/google-drive).

To learn more, read: [How to Send Exported WordPress Data to Zapier](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/external-app-integration/).

page_title: How to Export WordPress Posts
heading_path: Export WordPress Posts — Frequently Asked Questions
text: ## Export WordPress Posts — Frequently Asked Questions

page_title: How to Export WordPress Posts
heading_path: How Do I Export WordPress Posts to Excel?
text:
### How Do I Export WordPress Posts to Excel?

1. Go to **All Export > New Export** and select **Posts**.
2. Configure the export columns using our **Drag & Drop** interface.
3. In that same interface, change the **Export Type** to **Spreadsheet > Excel File (XLS)** or **Spreadsheet > Excel File (XLSX)**, depending on the version of Excel that you are using.
4. Run the export.
5. Verify the content of your export file.

page_title: How to Export WordPress Posts
heading_path: How Do I Export WordPress Posts to CSV?
text:
### How Do I Export WordPress Posts to CSV?

To export WordPress posts to CSV, just follow the steps in this guide, as CSV is the default file format for all exports using WP All Export.

page_title: How to Export WordPress Posts
heading_path: How Do I Extract a Single Post From WordPress?
text:
### How Do I Extract a Single Post From WordPress?

To extract a single post from WordPress, follow the steps in this guide but set a filter to isolate the desired post as described in the section [Add Filters to Export WordPress Posts Selectively](#export-wordpress-posts-filter).

page_title: How to Export WordPress Posts
heading_path: How Do I Export Selected Posts from WordPress?
text:
### How Do I Export Selected Posts from WordPress?

To export selected posts from WordPress, just follow the steps in this guide but [add a filter](#export-wordpress-posts-filter) that targets those specific posts. This is done in Step 1 at the bottom of the **New Export** page.

page_title: How to Export WordPress Posts
heading_path: How Do I Download a List of Posts From WordPress?
text:
### How Do I Download a List of Posts From WordPress?

Use the steps from this guide to export all WordPress posts from your WordPress site using the exact columns that you want in your list. If you only need a subset of posts, you can apply a filter as explained in the section [Add Filters to Export WordPress Posts Selectively](#export-wordpress-posts-filter).

page_title: How to Export WordPress Posts
heading_path: How Do I Export WordPress Posts Automatically?
text:
### How Do I Export WordPress Posts Automatically?

You can use our [automatic scheduling service](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/run-wordpress-export-on-a-schedule/) to run your WordPress posts export automatically on a predefined schedule. This can be scheduled directly from our interface. Alternatively, you can [manually set up cron jobs](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/scheduling-exports-using-cron-jobs/) on your server.

page_title: How to Export WordPress Posts
heading_path: How Do I Migrate Posts From One WordPress Site to Another?
text:
### How Do I Migrate Posts From One WordPress Site to Another?

1. Go to WP All Export and select **Migrate Posts** on the export site.
2. After the export is complete, download the **Bundle** file, which contains instructions on how to import the data.
3. Upload the bundle file on the import site.
4. Process the import to complete the migration.
5. Verify that the posts were migrated.

To see this process in more detail, refer to our [Migrate quickstart guide](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/migrate/).

page_title: How to Export WordPress Posts
heading_path: How Do I Import WordPress Posts?
text:
### How Do I Import WordPress Posts?

1. Navigate to **All Import › New Import**.
2. Choose how you want to import your data (i.e., upload a file, download a file from a URL, etc.)
3. Upload your file.
4. Choose **Posts** as your import target.
5. Use our **Drag & Drop** interface to map the incoming data elements to the post target fields.
6. Run the import.

For more information, see [How to Import WordPress Posts](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-import-wordpress-posts/).

page_title: How to Export WordPress Posts
heading_path: Can I Export WordPress Pages?
text:
### Can I Export WordPress Pages?

There are no major differences between exporting WordPress pages versus exporting posts. Just select **Pages** instead of **Posts** as the export type and WP All Export will adjust accordingly. See [How to Export WordPress Pages](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/how-to-export-wordpress-pages/).

page_title: How to Run Real-Time Exports
heading_path: How to Run Real-Time Exports
text:
To export posts one by one in real-time, create a new export and then enable **Export each post in real time as they are completed** on the **Export Settings** page.

Every time a new post is created, this export will run and create a new file containing only the post that was just created. You can then set up Zapier to send this data to over 5000+ apps, or create a custom solution using the WP All Export API.

page_title: How to Run Real-Time Exports
heading_path: Step 1: Create a New Export for WooCommerce Orders
text:
## Step 1: Create a New Export for WooCommerce Orders

Real-time exports are available for all new and existing exports, regardless of post type. In this example, we'll set up a real-time export for WooCommerce Orders.

First, go to **All Export › New Export** and select to export **WooCommerce Orders**:

![Real Time Export Create New](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/Real-Time-Export-Create-New-1024x758.jpg)

Click the **Customize Export File** button to continue.

page_title: How to Run Real-Time Exports
heading_path: Step 2: Select Your Export Columns
text:
## Step 2: Select Your Export Columns

Next, you'll see the **Drag & Drop** screen:

![Real Time Export Set Up Export Columns](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/Real-Time-Export-Set-Up-Export-Columns-1024x832.jpg)

In this screen, add and rearrange data columns that you wish to include in your export file.

In the **Export Type** section, you can choose to export any spreadsheet or XML feed.

When you're ready, click **Continue**.

page_title: How to Run Real-Time Exports
heading_path: Step 3: Enable Real-Time Exporting
text:
## Step 3: Enable Real-Time Exporting

Next up is the **Export Settings** screen. Here, you can enable real-time exports by clicking on **Export each WooCommerce Order in real time as they are completed**.

Once enabled, it looks like this:

![Export Each Order in Real Time as They are Completed](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Export-Each-Order-in-Real-Time-as-They-are-Completed-1024x661.jpg)

By enabling real-time exports for this order export, WP All Export will export every new order as it is created.

To finish setting up the export, just click on **Save & Run Export**. This will export the most recent WooCommerce order for testing purposes.

You can also switch an existing or previous WooCommerce orders export to real-time exporting. Simply navigate to **All Export › Manage Exports › Settings** and then enable real-time exports by checking the option **Export each WooCommerce Order in real time as they are completed.**

page_title: How to Run Real-Time Exports
heading_path: Step 4: Configure an Action to Trigger the Export
text:
## Step 4: Configure an Action to Trigger the Export

Now that we have created our real-time export, we need to configure an action to occur whenever our export runs. This can be done with Zapier or our API.

Zapier is a third-party service that can connect with WP All Export and allows sending the exported data to other services and apps. For example, you can send each exported WooCommerce Order via email, or add a new row to a Google Sheet with the order's data. Read more about Zapier here:

- [Integrate Zapier with WooCommerce for Real-Time Exports](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/integrate-zapier-with-woocommerce-for-real-time-exports/)
- [Add Exported WordPress Data to Google Sheets using Zapier](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/add-exported-wordpress-data-to-google-sheets-using-zapier/)
- [How to Send Exported WordPress Data to Zapier](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/external-app-integration/)
- [Send Exported WordPress Data via Email using Zapier](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/send-exported-wordpress-data-via-email-using-zapier/)
- [Send WordPress Export File to Google Drive using Zapier](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/send

page_title: How to Run Real-Time Exports
heading_path: Step 4: Configure an Action to Trigger the Export
text:
sing-zapier/)
- [Send WordPress Export File to Google Drive using Zapier](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/send-wordpress-export-file-to-google-drive-using-zapier/)

You can also use custom PHP code and our API to set up the action that will be triggered. This means that you can feed the exported data toward a custom code solution to process it in whichever way you need. Here's an example you can follow upon:

- [Real-Time Exports API Integration Example](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/real-time-exports-api-integration-example/)

page_title: How to Run Real-Time Exports
heading_path: How to Disable or Pause a Real-Time Export
text:
### How to Disable or Pause a Real-Time Export

You can disable real-time exports by unchecking that option on the **Settings** page for that specific export. The export will then go back to being a normal export.

You can also temporarily pause a real-time export from the **All Export › Manage Exports** page:

![Real-Time Export Enabled](https://www.wpallimport.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Real-Time-Export-Enabled-1024x288.jpg)

page_title: How to Run Real-Time Exports
heading_path: How To Access the Export File Using Custom Code
text:
### How To Access the Export File Using Custom Code

When real-time exports are enabled, a new file is created every time the export runs.

You can access the export with the [pmxe_after_export](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/action-reference/#pmxe_after_export) filter. Also, you can access the actual export file in 
```
$exportObj->options["current_filepath"]
```

.

Learn more about using custom code in our documentation. See [Custom PHP Functions](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/custom-wordpress-export-php/). You can also review our [Action Reference](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/action-reference/) to learn more about the available filters.

page_title: How to Run Real-Time Exports
heading_path: Can You Use Filtering Options With Real-Time Exports?
text:
### Can You Use Filtering Options With Real-Time Exports?

Yes, you can apply filtering options when creating the export and after it's created. You can also modify existing filters via **Manage Exports › Settings**.

Learn more about how to apply filtering options to your export in [Filter Exported WordPress Data](https://www.wpallimport.com/documentation/filter-exported-wordpress-data/).

page_title: How to Run Real-Time Exports
heading_path: Real-Time Exports — Frequently Asked Questions
text: ## Real-Time Exports — Frequently Asked Questions

page_title: How to Run Real-Time Exports
heading_path: What's the Difference Between a Normal Export and a Real-Time Export?
text:
### What's the Difference Between a Normal Export and a Real-Time Export?

A normal export runs manually or on a schedule and will export all records available in a single export file.

A real-time export runs every time a new record is created of that post type is created. For example, as soon as a new WooCommerce Order is completed on the site.

---
